Description/Asumptions:
- No authentication required — this is the entry point for unauthenticated users.
- Shown on app launch when no active session exists.
- Lets a new user choose their account type before registering: Employer or Candidate.
- Only two roles apply at registration: Employer and Candidate.
- Each role starts a distinct, role-specific registration flow.
- Account type selection cannot be bypassed — the user must pick a role to proceed.
- Existing users can jump to Log-in (2.1.2) from here.
- All connections must use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2 or later.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Welcome
- Create account as an Employer — Button — Primary — Navigates to Employer's registration (2.1.7)
- Create account as a Candidate — Button — Primary — Navigates to Candidate's registration (2.1.8)
- Already have an account? Log in — Hyperlink — Navigates to Log-in (2.1.2)
User Flow:
Screen: Welcome

Preconditions:
- No authentication required — this is the entry point for unauthenticated users.
- No active session exists. If a valid session exists ? system skips Welcome ? system navigates the user to Home Screen (2.3.1).
- No role restriction — any unauthenticated visitor can reach this screen.

User opens the app with no active session.
1. System displays the Welcome screen with both role options and the log-in link.
2. User taps [Create account as an Employer] ? system navigates to Employer's registration (2.1.7).
3. User taps [Create account as a Candidate] ? system navigates to Candidate's registration (2.1.8).
4. User taps [Already have an account? Log in] ? system navigates to Log-in (2.1.2).
5. The user must select one of the three actions; the system does NOT auto-select a role and does NOT allow bypassing role selection.

Description/Asumptions:
- No authentication required — entry point for users who already have an account.
- Reached from the Welcome screen [Log in] link or from any registration screen.
- Lets an existing user sign in with email address and password.
- Email + password is the only login method in MVP — no social or biometric login.
- The role is not selected at login. The login screen is identical for both roles (one email field, one password field, one submit button). After successful authentication the system resolves the account's stored role (Employer or Candidate) — returned in the session token / login response — and loads the matching experience. Each email maps to exactly one account with one fixed role (single-role rule enforced at registration, 2.1.7 / 2.1.8)
- Both roles share the same Home screen layout (2.3.1); the difference is role-adapted content, not a different screen: Employer Swipe shows Candidate listings (swipe right triggers a subscription check + contact reveal); Candidate Swipe shows Employer listings (swipe right is free and sends a notification only); [Manage subscription] in Settings is visible to Employers only; the listing form adapts by role (Employer: criteria form; Candidate: preferences + contact details + consent)
- Phone verification is a hard, server-side gate for Candidates: if an authenticated Candidate has phone_verified = false, every protected endpoint returns 403 with a redirect to the Phone Verification screen (2.1.8) until verification completes
- Failed login returns a generic message that does not reveal whether the email or the password was wrong.
- Progressive lockout applies : after 5 consecutive failed attempts the account is locked for 15 minutes; after 10 consecutive failed attempts (across lockout cycles) it is locked for 1 hour and a suspicious-activity email is sent to the account owner.
- A successful login OR a successful password reset resets the failed-attempt counter to zero.
- All connections must use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2 or later; session tokens use TLS 1.2+.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Log-in
- Email address — Text field — Required — Max 100 characters — Must follow email format — Accepts letters (A-Z, a-z), numbers (0-9), special characters — Red border displayed on empty/invalid field when validation is triggered
- Password — Text field (masked) — Required — Min 8 char — Max 64 char — Show/hide toggle — Accepts letters (A-Z, a-z), numbers (0-9), special characters — Red border on empty/invalid field when validation is triggered
- Log in — Button — Primary — Disabled until both fields contain input
- Forgot password? — Hyperlink — Navigates to Password reset (2.1.4)
- Don't have an account? Register — Hyperlink — Navigates to Welcome screen (2.1.1)
User Flow:
Screen: Log-in

Preconditions:
- No authentication required — this is an entry point for unauthenticated users.
- User must already have an account (Employer or Candidate).
- If a valid session already exists ? system skips Log-in ? system navigates to Home Screen (2.3.1).

User arrives from Welcome screen or a registration screen.
1. System displays the Log-in screen.
2. User enters Email address and Password.
3. User taps [Log in] ? system validates input:
   3.1. Validate required fields:
    + If Email is empty ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? system displays error: "Email is required."
    + If Password is empty ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? system displays error: "Password is required."
   3.2. Validate email format (system checks input against standard email format — contains @ and a valid domain):
    + If format is invalid ? system does NOT query the backend ? system displays error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   3.3. Validate credentials (backend compares email + password against stored User records):
    + If credentials are valid ? backend returns success ? system resets the failed-attempt counter to zero ? system creates an authentication session ? system stores the token securely ? the backend returns the account's role (Employer or Candidate) in the session token / login response ? system loads the role-appropriate experience and navigates the user to Home Screen (2.3.1).
       - The role is NOT chosen at login and the system does NOT show a role picker — the login screen is identical for both roles; each email maps to exactly one account with one fixed role (single-role rule enforced at registration, 2.1.7 / 2.1.8).
       - Both roles land on the same Home screen layout (2.3.1); content is role-adapted, not a separate screen.
       - If the account is an Employer ? system enables Employer-adapted content: Employer Swipe (Candidate listings; swipe right triggers subscription check + contact reveal), Listing management (criteria form), and [Manage subscription] visible in Settings.
       - If the account is a Candidate and phone_verified = true ? system enables Candidate-adapted content: Candidate Swipe (Employer listings; swipe right is free + notification only) and Candidate listing/preferences set-up.
       - If the account is a Candidate and phone_verified = false ? system does NOT grant access to Home/Swipe/Chat/Settings ? system immediately redirects to the Phone Verification screen (2.1.8). The gate is enforced server-side: every authenticated API call checks phone_verified and returns 403 with a redirect instruction while it is false, until verification completes.
    + If email is not found OR password does not match ? backend returns failure ? system does NOT create a session ? system does NOT navigate ? system increments the failed-attempt counter ? system displays generic error: "Invalid email or password."
    + If the backend is unreachable ? system does NOT authenticate ? credentials remain unchecked ? system does NOT increment the counter ? system displays error: "Network error. Please check your connection and try again."
   3.4. Validate lockout state (backend checks the consecutive-failed-attempt counter for this account):
    + If the account has reached 5 consecutive failed attempts ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? account is locked for 15 minutes ? system displays error: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes, or reset your password."
    + If the account has reached 10 consecutive failed attempts (across lockout cycles) ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? account is locked for 1 hour ? backend sends a suspicious-activity warning email to the account owner ? system displays error: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 60 minutes, or reset your password."
    + A successful login or a completed password reset (2.1.4) resets the counter to zero.
4. User taps [Forgot password?] ? system navigates to Password reset (2.1.4).
5. User taps [Don't have an account? Register] ? system navigates to Welcome screen (2.1.1).

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user (Employer or Candidate).
- Triggered from the Settings screen [Log out] action.
- Ends the current session, clears all local tokens, and invalidates the server-side token.
- A token revocation endpoint is available; logout calls it to invalidate the server-side token.
- No global multi-device session management in MVP — logout affects the current device session.
- Tokens are invalidated within 1 second of logout; any API call made after logout returns an unauthorized error.
- This is a destructive/irreversible action, so a confirmation prompt is required before logging out.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Settings (logout entry point) + confirmation popup
- Log out — Button — Danger — Accessible from the Settings screen

Popup: Log out confirmation
- Confirmation message — Text — "Are you sure you want to log out?"
- Confirm — Button — Danger
- Cancel — Button — Secondary
User Flow:
Screen: Settings — Logout

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated (Employer or Candidate).
- User must be on the Settings screen.
- Token revocation endpoint must be configured and reachable.

User is on the Settings screen.
1. User taps [Log out] ? system opens the Log out confirmation popup.
2. System displays message: "Are you sure you want to log out?" with [Confirm] and [Cancel].
3. User taps [Cancel] ? system closes the popup ? system does NOT clear tokens ? user remains logged in on the Settings screen.
4. User taps [Confirm] ? system requests token invalidation from the backend revocation endpoint:
   4.1. If the backend confirms revocation ? backend invalidates the server-side token and revokes the refresh token ? system clears all local tokens and session data ? system navigates the user to the Welcome screen (2.1.1).
   4.2. If the backend is unreachable ? system still clears all local tokens and session data ? system navigates to the Welcome screen (2.1.1) ? the server-side token is queued for invalidation on next reachability (best-effort), so the user is signed out locally regardless.
5. After logout, any subsequent API call made with the old token ? backend returns an unauthorized error ? system does NOT serve protected content.

Description/Asumptions:
- No authentication required — for users who have forgotten their password.
- Triggered from the Login screen [Forgot password?] link.
- Two screens: (1) request a reset link by email, (2) set a new password via the emailed link.
- Email service is available for sending reset links.
- The reset link expires 30 minutes after generation and is single-use — once clicked it cannot be reused even within the 30-minute window.
- After expiry or use the user must request a new reset link.
- For security, the request screen never reveals whether an email is registered — it always shows the same neutral confirmation, and response time is kept consistent to avoid timing-based account enumeration.
- After a successful reset the old password is invalid.
- New password must meet the same policy as registration: minimum 8 characters, including letters, numbers, at least one uppercase letter, and one special character.D3

- Email address — Text field — Required — Max 100 characters — Must follow email format — Red border on empty/invalid field when validation is triggered
- Send reset link — Button — Primary — Disabled until a value is entered
- Can't find your account? Contact support — Hyperlink — Opens the device's native email client pre-filled to the support address (same channel as Contact us, 2.6.9) —      
Pre-filled Subject: "Account recovery — can't find my account" — Pre-filled Body template: "I'm having trouble finding my account. My email address is: [user fills in]. My issue is: [user fills in]." — Label includes "Contact support" so the user knows it opens an email, not a help page

Screen 2 - Set new password (opened via email link)
- New password — Text field (masked) — Required — Min 8 char — Max 64 char — Must include letters, numbers, at least one uppercase letter, and one special character — Show/hide toggle
- Confirm new password — Text field (masked) — Required — Must match New password
- Reset password — Button — Primary — Disabled until both fields filled
User Flow:
Screen: Password reset request

Preconditions:
- No authentication required — entry point for unauthenticated users.
- Email service must be configured and reachable.

User taps [Forgot password?] on the Login screen.
1. System displays the Password reset request screen.
2. User enters Email address ? user taps [Send reset link] ? system validates:
   2.1. If Email is empty ? system does NOT send ? system displays error: "Email is required."
   2.2. If email format is invalid ? system does NOT send ? system displays error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   2.3. If format is valid ? system requests the email service to send a reset link to that address:
    + System always displays the same neutral confirmation regardless of whether the email is registered, and keeps response time consistent to prevent timing-based enumeration: "If an account with this email exists, a password reset link has been sent."
    + If the email is registered ? backend generates a single-use reset token that expires 30 minutes after generation ? email service sends the reset link.
    + If the email service is unreachable ? system does NOT confirm send ? system displays error: "Unable to send reset link right now. Please try again."
   2.4. User taps [Can't find your account? Contact support] ? system opens the device's native email client pre-filled to the support address (same email-based channel as Contact us, 2.6.9), with Subject "Account recovery — can't find my account" and a body template prompting the user for their email and issue. The pre-filled subject lets support filter account-recovery requests into a priority queue, separate from general 2.6.9 enquiries. No authentication is required — this is a logged-out screen, so the email client opens directly.

Screen: Set new password (opened via emailed link)
3. User opens the reset link ? system validates the reset token:
   3.1. If the token is valid, unused, and within 30 minutes of generation ? system displays the Set new password screen.
   3.2. If the token has expired (older than 30 minutes) or was already used ? system does NOT display the form ? system displays error: "This link has expired. Please request a new password reset."
4. User enters New password and Confirm new password ? user taps [Reset password] ? system validates:
   4.1. If New password does not meet policy (min 8 chars, letters, numbers, 1 uppercase, 1 special) ? system does NOT update ? system displays error: "Password must be at least 8 characters and include a letter, a number, an uppercase letter, and a special character."
   4.2. If Confirm new password does not match ? system does NOT update ? system displays error: "Passwords do not match."
   4.3. If valid ? backend updates the password, invalidates the old password, and marks the reset token used ? system displays success message: "Your password has been reset successfully." ? system navigates the user to Log-in (2.1.2).

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any logged-in user; reached via Settings > Manage Profile > Change password.
- Lets a logged-in user change their current password from within the app.
- Old (current) password must be validated before the new one is accepted.
- New password enforces the same policy as signup (min 8 characters, letters, numbers, 1 uppercase, 1 special character).
- After a successful change the user is logged out from all devices (all tokens invalidated).
- Old password is always available for validation; password update API with token invalidation is used.

- Current password — Text field (masked) — Required — Show/hide toggle
- New password — Text field (masked) — Required — Min 8 char — Max 64 char — Must include letters, numbers, at least one uppercase letter, and one special character — Show/hide toggle
- Confirm new password — Text field (masked) — Required — Must match New password

Screen: Change password

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated.
- User must be on Settings > Manage Profile > Change password.

User navigates to Settings > Manage Profile > Change password.
1. System displays the Change password screen; [Save] is disabled until all three fields are filled.
2. User enters Current password, New password, Confirm new password ? user taps [Save] ? system validates:
   2.1. Validate current password (backend checks the entered current password against the stored credential):
    + If the current password is incorrect ? backend returns failure ? system does NOT update ? system displays error: "Your current password is incorrect."
   2.2. Validate new password policy:
    + If New password does not meet policy ? system does NOT update ? system displays error: "Password must be at least 8 characters and include a letter, a number, an uppercase letter, and a special character."
    + If New password is identical to the current password ? system does NOT update ? system displays error: "New password must be different from your current password."
   2.3. Validate confirmation:
    + If Confirm new password does not match New password ? system does NOT update ? system displays error: "Passwords do not match."
   2.4. If all checks pass ? backend updates the password and invalidates all existing tokens across all devices ? system displays success message: "Password changed successfully. Please log in again." ? system clears the local session ? system navigates the user to Log-in (2.1.2).
3. User taps [Cancel] ? system discards input ? system does NOT change the password ? system returns to Manage Profile.

Description/Asumptions:
- No authentication required — reached from the Welcome screen [Create account as an Employer].
- Lets a company create an Employer account with company details and credentials.
- Email must be unique across all account types.
- A user already registered as a Candidate cannot register as an Employer (and vice versa).
- Both the age-confirmation and accuracy-confirmation checkboxes are mandatory; account creation is blocked until both are ticked.
- An auto-renewal disclosure (passive text) informs the user that the Employer subscription auto-renews and can be managed/cancelled in App Store / Google Play; proceeding constitutes acknowledgement per store transparency guidelines.
- No further identity verification is required beyond the mandatory fields.
- Anti-bot CAPTCHA (2.1.8) must be satisfied before submission; password policy = min 8 chars, letters, numbers, 1 uppercase, 1 special.
- Auto-renewal disclosure text content is supplied by the Client/legal advisor.

- Password — Text field (masked) — Required — Min 8 char — Max 64 char — Must include letters, numbers, 1 uppercase, 1 special character — Show/hide toggle

Screen: Employer's registration

Preconditions:
- No authentication required — entry point for unauthenticated users.
- User arrived from Welcome screen [Create account as an Employer] (2.1.1).
- CAPTCHA / WAF anti-bot protection (2.1.8) must be available.

User taps [Create account as an Employer] on the Welcome screen.
1. System displays the Employer's registration form; [Create account] is disabled until all mandatory fields are filled and both checkboxes are ticked.
2. User enters Company name, Email address, Password, ticks both confirmation checkboxes, and reads the auto-renewal disclosure.
3. User taps [Create account] ? system runs anti-bot verification first (see 2.1.8):
   3.1. If CAPTCHA/WAF check fails ? system does NOT submit the registration ? system prompts the user to retry.
   3.2. If anti-bot check passes ? system proceeds to validation.
4. System validates the form:
   4.1. Required fields — if Company name, Email, or Password is empty, or either checkbox is unticked ? system does NOT create the account ? system displays the relevant error (e.g. "Company name is required.", "You must confirm you are 18 or above.").
   4.2. Email format — if invalid ? system does NOT query the backend ? system displays error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   4.3. Password policy — if New password fails policy ? system does NOT create the account ? system displays error: "Password must be at least 8 characters and include a letter, a number, an uppercase letter, and a special character."
   4.4. Email uniqueness (backend checks the email against all existing account records of every type):
    + If the email already exists as an Employer ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "An account with this email already exists."
    + If the email already exists as a Candidate ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "This email is already registered as a Candidate account."
    + If the backend is unreachable ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "Unable to verify email right now. Please try again."
   4.5. If all checks pass ? backend creates the Employer account ? system displays success message: "Your Employer account has been created." ? system logs the user in and navigates to Home Screen (2.3.1).
5. User taps [Already have an account? Log in] ? system navigates to Log-in (2.1.2).

Description/Asumptions:
- No authentication required — reached from the Welcome screen [Create account as a Candidate].
- Two screens: (1) registration form, (2) phone verification via SMS OTP.
- Registration form collects: First name, Last name, Email address, Phone number, Password, plus 2 mandatory confirmation checkboxes and the submit button.
- Email must be unique across all account types; a Candidate cannot also register as an Employer (and vice versa).
- Both the age-confirmation and accuracy-confirmation checkboxes are mandatory; account creation is blocked until both are ticked.
- After account creation the candidate must verify their phone number with an OTP sent via SMS.
- OTP is sent automatically after registration (user need not request it manually on first entry); OTP is a 6-digit, number-only code; OTP expires after 10 minutes.
- Maximum 3 resend attempts per session — the account is not locked, but further resends are blocked until the counter resets. The resend counter resets when: the user fully closes and reopens the app (process terminated, not just backgrounded), OR 24 hours have elapsed since the first resend, OR the user abandons and starts a new registration from the Welcome screen. The counter does NOT reset on navigating Back and returning to the OTP screen, on backgrounding the app, or on OTP expiry.
- Verification is required before the candidate can access any other app feature.
- Anti-bot CAPTCHA (2.1.8) must be satisfied before submission; password policy = min 8 chars, letters, numbers, 1 uppercase, 1 special.

- Last name — Text field — Required — Max 50 characters — Accepts letters (A-Z, a-z), spaces, hyphen, apostrophe
- Email address — Text field — Required — Max 100 characters — Must follow email format
- Phone number — Text field — Required — Numeric with country code — Used for SMS OTP
- Password — Text field (masked) — Required — Min 8 char — Max 64 char — Must include letters, numbers, 1 uppercase, 1 special character — 

Show/hide toggle
- I confirm that I am 18 years old or above — Checkbox — Required
- I confirm that the information I have provided is accurate and complete — Checkbox — Required
- Create account — Button — Primary — Disabled until all fields are filled AND both checkboxes are ticked
- Already have an account? Log in — Hyperlink — Navigates to Log-in (2.1.2)

- OTP code — Text field — Required — Exactly 6 digits — Numeric only
- Verify — Button — Primary — Disabled until 6 digits entered
- Resend code — Button — Secondary — Disabled for 60 seconds after each send; blocked after 3 sends per session
- Back — Button — Secondary — Returns to the registration form
User Flow:
Screen: Candidate registration

Preconditions:
- No authentication required — entry point for unauthenticated users.
- User arrived from Welcome screen [Create account as a Candidate] (2.1.1).
- SMS/OTP service and CAPTCHA/WAF anti-bot protection (2.1.8) must be available.

User taps [Create account as a Candidate] on the Welcome screen.
1. System displays the registration form; [Create account] disabled until all fields filled and both checkboxes ticked.
2. User enters First name, Last name, Email, Phone number, Password and ticks both checkboxes.
3. User taps [Create account] ? system runs anti-bot verification first (see 2.1.8):
   3.1. If CAPTCHA/WAF fails ? system does NOT submit ? system prompts the user to retry.
   3.2. If it passes ? system proceeds to validation.
4. System validates the form:
   4.1. Required fields — if any field is empty or either checkbox is unticked ? system does NOT create the account ? system displays the relevant error.
   4.2. Email format — if invalid ? system does NOT proceed ? system displays error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   4.3. Password policy — if it fails policy ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "Password must be at least 8 characters and include a letter, a number, an uppercase letter, and a special character."
   4.4. Email uniqueness (backend checks email against all account types):
    + If email already exists as a Candidate ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "An account with this email already exists."
    + If email already exists as an Employer ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "This email is already registered as an Employer account."
    + If backend unreachable ? system does NOT create ? system displays error: "Unable to verify email right now. Please try again."
   4.5. If all checks pass ? backend creates the Candidate account ? system automatically sends a 6-digit OTP via the SMS service to the registered phone number ? system navigates to the Phone verification screen.

Screen: Phone verification
5. System displays the masked/registered phone number and the OTP input; the OTP was sent automatically — the user need not request it on first entry.
6. User enters the 6-digit OTP ? user taps [Verify] ? system sends the entered code to the backend verification endpoint:
   6.1. If the code matches and the OTP has not expired (? 10 minutes old) ? backend returns success ? system marks the phone number as verified ? system displays success message: "Your account has been verified." ? system logs the user in and navigates to Home Screen (2.3.1).
   6.2. If the code does not match ? backend returns failure ? system does NOT verify ? system displays error: "Incorrect code. Please try again."
   6.3. If the OTP has expired (older than 10 minutes) ? backend returns expiry ? system does NOT verify ? system displays error: "This code has expired. Please request a new one."
   6.4. If the SMS/verification service is unreachable ? system does NOT verify ? system displays error: "Unable to verify right now. Please check your connection and try again."
7. User taps [Resend code]:
   7.1. If fewer than 3 sends have been used in the current session ? system requests a new OTP from the SMS service ? [Resend code] is disabled for 60 seconds.
   7.2. If 3 sends have already been used in the current session ? system does NOT send another code ? system displays message: "You have reached the maximum number of resend attempts. Please try again later." (account is NOT locked).
   7.3. The resend counter resets when the user fully closes and reopens the app, OR 24 hours have elapsed since the first resend, OR the user starts a new registration from the Welcome screen. It does NOT reset on tapping [Back] and returning, on backgrounding the app, or on OTP expiry.
8. User taps [Back] ? system returns to the registration form.
9. Until phone verification is completed, system does NOT grant access to any other app feature — any attempt to navigate elsewhere returns the user to the Phone verification screen.

Description/Asumptions:
- This is not a standalone screen — anti-bot verification operates within the registration flows (Employer 2.1.7 and Candidate 2.1.8).
- Purpose: confirm the registrant is a human, not a bot, to prevent spam accounts and abuse.
- Two independent layers: (1) client-side invisible CAPTCHA on the registration form, (2) infrastructure-level bot filtering via AWS WAF at the API gateway.
- CAPTCHA verification runs server-side before account creation is processed.
- If CAPTCHA fails, the registration request is not submitted and the user is prompted to retry.
- CAPTCHA applies to the registration flow only — not to login.
- A request must pass BOTH CAPTCHA and WAF validation for account creation to occur.
- A third-party invisible CAPTCHA service is used: Google reCAPTCHA v3 (invisible, score-based) as primary; if the v3 score is below 0.5, fall back to an interactive reCAPTCHA v2 challenge ("I'm not a robot" / image selection). hCaptcha is documented as a swap-in alternative in the technical architecture so the integration can change without redesign. Must be compatible with both iOS and Android native app environments.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This feature is embedded within the Employer's registration (2.1.7) and Candidate's registration (2.1.8) screens.
- CAPTCHA challenge widget — Component — Google reCAPTCHA v3 (invisible, score-based, no user friction) — Integrated into both registration screens — Must run before the registration request is submitted — Falls back to an interactive reCAPTCHA v2 challenge when the v3 score is below 0.5
- AWS WAF rules — Infrastructure component (no UI) — Applied at the API gateway to block known bot traffic patterns and malicious request signatures
User Flow:
System Process / Embedded verification — operates within the registration flow (2.1.7 / 2.1.8).

Preconditions:
- Triggered when the user submits a registration form (Employer or Candidate).
- A third-party CAPTCHA service must be configured; AWS WAF must be active at the API gateway.
- Does not apply to Log-in (2.1.2).

1. User taps [Create account] on a registration screen ? system silently runs the invisible CAPTCHA challenge (no visible prompt).
2. System submits the registration request only after obtaining a CAPTCHA token; the request passes through the API gateway where AWS WAF inspects it:
   2.1. WAF layer — gateway evaluates the request against bot/malicious-signature rules:
    + If the request matches a blocked pattern ? gateway rejects it ? system does NOT create the account ? registration request is not processed.
   2.2. CAPTCHA layer — backend verifies the reCAPTCHA v3 token server-side and reads the returned score:
    + If the v3 score is at or above 0.5 ? CAPTCHA passes ? proceed.
    + If the v3 score is below 0.5 ? system presents an interactive reCAPTCHA v2 challenge to the user:
       - If the user completes the v2 challenge successfully ? CAPTCHA passes ? proceed.
       - If the user fails or dismisses the v2 challenge ? system does NOT submit/complete registration ? system prompts the user to retry: "We couldn't verify that you're human. Please try again."
   2.3. If BOTH the WAF check and the server-side CAPTCHA verification pass ? system proceeds with account creation in the parent registration flow (2.1.7 / 2.1.8).
3. System does NOT apply CAPTCHA to login; ongoing verification for Candidates relies on phone OTP and for Employers on email uniqueness.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any newly registered user (Employer or Candidate); shown on first login after registration.
- Provides a guided first-use experience: a 2D animated mascot introduces the app's main functionalities.
- Tutorial covers: how to create a Listing (form + video), how to use the swipe feed, how to view favourites, how to access chat/Discussion, and how to manage profile and settings.
- Each step shows the mascot with a speech bubble (instructional text) and a spotlight/highlight on the relevant UI element, plus [Next] and [Skip tutorial].
- Shown only once per account on first login; completion status is stored per user so it is not shown again on later sessions.
- Tapping [Skip tutorial] dismisses the mascot and allows free navigation — the tutorial must never block app use, so [Skip tutorial] is always available.
- The tutorial can be re-accessed later from Settings.
- The tutorial flow is role-specific: the Employer tutorial emphasises the swipe feed and subscription; the Candidate tutorial emphasises listing creation and video recording.
- Mascot character design, animation style, and instructional copy are defined during UX/UI design; mascot assets and their rendering/sequencing are delivered as design assets and implemented per 2.2.2.

- UI spotlight/highlight — Display — Dims the background and highlights the relevant UI element for the step
- Next — Button — Primary — Proceeds to the next tutorial step
- Skip tutorial — Button — Secondary — Always available — Dismisses the mascot and allows free navigation
- Re-access tutorial — Entry point in Settings — Allows the user to replay the tutorial later

Screen: Onboarding tutorial (overlay)

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated and on their first login after registration (Employer or Candidate).
- For Candidates, phone verification (2.1.8) must be complete before the app — and therefore the tutorial — is reachable.
- The tutorial-completed flag for this account must be false (otherwise it is not shown).
- If the tutorial-completed flag is true ? system does NOT show the tutorial ? user proceeds directly to Home Screen (2.3.1).

User completes registration and enters the app for the first time.
1. System checks the tutorial-completed flag for the account:
   1.1. If false ? system starts the role-appropriate tutorial overlay (Employer or Candidate variant).
   1.2. If true ? system does NOT start the tutorial ? user lands on Home Screen (2.3.1).
2. System displays the first step: mascot + localised speech bubble + spotlight on the relevant UI element, with [Next] and [Skip tutorial].
3. User taps [Next] ? system advances to the next step ? system repeats until the final step.

4. User taps [Skip tutorial] at any step ? system does NOT show the remaining steps ? system marks the tutorial-completed flag true ? system dismisses the overlay ? user has free navigation on Home Screen (2.3.1).

Preconditions:

    + If the server returns the assets ? system caches and renders them.

Description/Asumptions:

- Lets an Employer define hiring criteria via a structured form, then review and either save as draft or proceed to video recording.
- Hiring position (job title) is a required free-text field and serves as the listing's headline/title shown to candidates; it is display-only and not used as a matching key (matching is driven by Field of Activity).
- Required to activate the feed: Hiring Position, Desired profile (gender preference), Age Range, Company Location, Search Radius, Field of Activity, and Contract Type.
- Optional (do not block): Workload percentage, Required language skills, Job start date.
- Value lists: Field of Activity = 20 predefined values; Contract Type = 6 predefined values; Required languages = predefined multi-select with an "Other" free-text option. Age range 18–65 (default full range). Workload 0–100% in 10% steps. Search radius 5–200 km in 5 km steps, default 50 km, with a real-time map circle.
- After input the Employer reviews a read-only summary and can edit, save as draft, or confirm and proceed to record video.
- Location is map-based (see Map & Geolocation 2.4.5 for radius); Field of Activity and Contract Type are predefined lists.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user creating/updating a listing video (Employer or Candidate).
- Triggered by [Proceed to record video] from the Listing summary, or [Re-record] from the post-recording preview.
- Lets the user record a short introduction video with the device camera, with the option to apply one of 10–15 static professional backgrounds.
- Hard 30-second recording cap — recording auto-stops at 30 seconds and cannot be extended.
- Front camera is the default; [Flip camera] toggles front/rear.
- Background selection is optional and happens BEFORE recording starts on the camera preview screen; the first option is always "None" (natural background, default selected). Selecting a thumbnail applies it in real time to the live preview. Once recording starts the selected background is locked and cannot change mid-recording; it is applied at recording time only, not retroactively.
- Camera and microphone permissions are requested on first use.
- 10 static professional backgrounds (final count) are bundled with the app binary at build time (not downloaded at runtime): 3 solid colours (white, light grey, soft blue), 3 office environments (modern office, meeting room, coworking space), 2 abstract/gradient (professional gradient, subtle pattern), 2 outdoor (urban skyline, park/nature). Static images only — no animated/video backgrounds (out of scope for MVP).
- Background replacement uses real-time person segmentation: iOS via Apple Vision (PersonSegmentationRequest), Android via Google ML Kit Selfie Segmentation, at ~30fps for live preview and recording.
- Device-compatibility fallback: if the device does not support real-time segmentation (minimum iOS 15+ with A14 chip / Android 10+ with ML Kit), the background option is simply hidden — the user records with their natural background and no error message is shown.
- After recording the user can preview, re-record, save as draft, or submit the listing for review.
- Listings are moderated manually by an Admin (no AI scanning): [Submit for review] sets the listing to Pending Review and sends the video to the Admin moderation queue (3.3.1). An Admin reviews it (3.3.3) and either approves (listing Published, visible in the feed; user notified and matching employers notified) or rejects (listing Rejected; user notified, can re-record/upload). Rejection reasons are not disclosed to the user.
- Both Employers and Candidates can keep using search/swipe without completing a listing video.
- If a teleprompter script is active (2.2.7), it scrolls as an overlay during recording.

- Background selector — Option Cards (thumbnail row below the preview) — Optional — First option always "None" (natural background, default selected) + 10 static professional backgrounds — Selection applies in real time to the live preview — Hidden entirely on devices that don't support real-time segmentation — Locked once recording starts

- Re-record — Button — Secondary
- Save as draft — Button — Secondary

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated and have an in-progress listing (from 2.2.3 / 2.2.4) or be re-recording an existing one.
- Device must have a working camera and microphone.
- Camera and microphone permissions are required.

User taps [Proceed to record video] from the Listing summary (or [Re-record] from preview).
1. System requests camera and microphone permissions from the device OS (first use):
   1.1. System requests camera + microphone access ? device OS presents its native permission dialog:
    + If the user grants both ? system shows the full-screen live camera preview (front camera default).
    + If the user denies camera or microphone ? system does NOT open the camera ? system displays a message explaining the permissions are required and offers a shortcut to device Settings: "Camera and microphone access are needed to record your video. Please enable them in Settings."
    + If permissions were previously denied permanently ? system does NOT prompt again ? system shows the same Settings guidance.
2. System checks device support for real-time segmentation, then displays the live preview, countdown timer (30s), [Record], [Flip camera]:
   2.1. If the device supports real-time segmentation (iOS 15+ with A14 / Android 10+ with ML Kit) ? system shows the background selector thumbnail row with "None" selected by default plus the 10 static backgrounds.
   2.2. If the device does NOT support real-time segmentation ? system does NOT show the background selector at all (no error message) ? the user records with their natural background.
3. User optionally taps a background thumbnail ? system applies it in real time to the live preview via person segmentation (iOS Vision / Android ML Kit); default is "None". User taps [Flip camera] ? system toggles front/rear.
4. User taps [Record] ? system locks the selected background (cannot change mid-recording) ? system starts recording and counts down from 30 seconds; if a script is active (2.2.7), the teleprompter scrolls during recording:
   4.1. User taps to stop before 30s ? system stops recording ? system proceeds to the post-recording preview.
   4.2. If the 30-second cap is reached ? system auto-stops recording (cannot be extended) ? system proceeds to the post-recording preview.
   4.3. The selected background is baked in at recording time only; it is NOT applied retroactively to an already-recorded clip.
5. Post-recording preview — system plays back the recorded video with [Re-record], [Save as draft], [Submit for review].
6. User taps [Re-record] ? system discards the current clip ? system returns to step 2.
7. User taps [Save as draft] ? system saves the listing + video in Draft status ? system displays success message: "Listing saved as draft." ? system navigates to the user's drafts (2.6.5).
8. User taps [Submit for review] ? system sets the listing to Pending Review and sends the video to the Admin moderation queue (3.3.1 / decision in 3.3.3) ? system shows an on-screen confirmation (not a push, since the user just acted): "Your listing has been submitted for review. You will be notified once it is approved." ? the listing shows a "Pending Review" badge in Listing management (2.6.3). System does NOT publish or show it in the feed yet.
   8.1. If an Admin approves the video ? listing becomes Published and visible in the feed ? user is notified via Video validation notice (2.7.6) ? matching Employers are notified (2.7.3).
   8.2. If an Admin rejects the video ? listing becomes Rejected ? user is notified via 2.7.6 (reason NOT disclosed) and can return here (2.2.5) or to Video Upload (2.2.6) to provide a replacement, which re-enters the queue as Pending Review.
   8.3. System does NOT send any "still pending" reminder to the user; queue age is an Admin-side metric only.
9. The user may exit this screen and continue using search/swipe without completing the video.

Screen: Prepare script

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated and in the listing-video flow.

User taps [Prepare script] before starting recording.

2. User taps [Generate script with AI] ? system checks profile completeness:
   2.1. If the profile is not sufficiently completed ? system does NOT call the AI service ? system displays message: "Add a few profile details first so we can generate a script for you."
   2.2. If the profile is sufficiently completed ? system submits the relevant profile data to the LLM script service (GPT-4.1 mini):
    + If the service returns a script ? system populates the text area with the generated script for the user to edit.
    + If the service returns an error or times out ? system does NOT populate a script ? system displays message: "Couldn't generate a script right now. You can write your own or try again." ? the user can still type a script manually.
3. User optionally edits the text area or types a custom script; user adjusts [Scroll speed].
4. User taps [Start recording with teleprompter] ? system marks one script active for this recording session ? system navigates to Camera Recording (2.2.5), where the script scrolls as a teleprompter overlay at the chosen speed.
   4.1. Only one script is allowed per recording session; starting a new recording session resets the active script.
5. User taps [Skip] ? system proceeds to Camera Recording (2.2.5) with no teleprompter overlay.

Description/Asumptions:

- Provides the main entry points to the app's core features.
- Both roles land on the same Home layout with role-adapted content (see 2.1.2): the listing-creation entry routes to the Employer or Candidate listing flow per role.
- [Post a listing] is optional — both Employers and Candidates can use Swipe to browse without posting a listing.
- All features accessible from the Home screen are available without a subscription; only the Employer contact-detail reveal on swipe-right requires a subscription (2.4.1).
- Persistent bottom navigation with four tabs: Home, Chat, Notifications (with unread badge), Settings.

Screen name: Home
- Post a listing — Button — Primary — Navigates to the role-appropriate listing flow (Employer 2.2.3 / Candidate 2.2.4) — Optional action
- Go to Swipe — Button — Primary — Navigates to the swipe feed (Employer 2.4.1 / Candidate 2.4.2)
- Bottom navigation bar — Component — Persistent across main screens
  + Home — Tab — Active state on this screen
  + Chat — Tab — Navigates to Conversation list (2.5.1)
  + Notifications — Tab — Navigates to Notifications; shows an unread count badge
  + Settings — Tab — Navigates to Profile & Account Settings (2.6)
User Flow:
Screen: Home Screen

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated (Employer or Candidate).
- For Candidates, phone verification (2.1.8) must be complete; otherwise the verification gate redirects to 2.1.8 (per 2.1.2).
- No subscription is required to reach or use the Home screen.

User completes login or taps the Home tab.
1. System loads the Home screen with the role-adapted content and the four-tab bottom navigation; the Home tab shows the active state.
2. System checks for unread notifications:
   2.1. If unread notifications exist ? system shows an unread count badge on the Notifications tab.
   2.2. If none ? system does NOT show a badge.
3. User taps [Post a listing] ? system navigates to the role-appropriate listing flow (Employer 2.2.3 / Candidate 2.2.4). This action is optional and is not required to use Swipe.
4. User taps [Go to Swipe] ? system navigates to the role-appropriate swipe feed (Employer 2.4.1 / Candidate 2.4.2).
5. User taps a bottom-navigation tab:

   5.2. [Notifications] ? system navigates to Notifications and clears the unread badge as items are seen.
   5.3. [Settings] ? system navigates to Profile & Account Settings (2.6).
   5.4. [Home] ? system stays on / returns to the Home screen.
6. All entry points here function without a subscription; the only subscription gate in the app is the Employer contact reveal on swipe-right (2.4.1).

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to authenticated Employers; entered from Home (2.3.1) or after completing the Employer listing setup (2.2.3).
- Lets an Employer browse Candidate listings in a swipe-card interface: swipe right to express interest, swipe left to skip, or bookmark to Favourites.
- Each card shows the Candidate's introduction video and basic listing information only.
- Personal candidate information (name, email, phone) must NOT be visible in the card stack — it is revealed only after the subscription-gated contact unlock.
- The swipe gesture itself is unlimited and free; only the contact-detail reveal requires an active subscription.
- Swipe right with an active subscription ? the Employer immediately sees the candidate's contact details and a 1-1 chat opens automatically (2.5.2).
- Swipe right without a subscription ? the Employer is directed to the purchase subscription screen (2.6.13 / 2.6.14); on completed purchase they return to Swipe and the contact is revealed.
- Candidate contact details are pre-consented at listing creation (2.2.4) — no additional candidate approval is needed.
- The Employer can adjust their location search radius here to change geographic coverage (radius logic per 2.4.5).
- Cards load in batches of ~20; already-seen cards are excluded from future feed loads.
- When no candidate profiles match the Employer's active criteria, an empty state is shown.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Employer Swipe feed
- Swipe card — Display — Full-screen — Shows: Candidate introduction video (autoplay/tap to play) + basic listing info (no personal contact data)
  + Candidate video — Display — Plays the candidate's introduction video
  + Basic listing info — Display — Field of activity, contract type, preferred region (general), availability — NO name/email/phone
- Swipe right — Gesture — Express interest ? subscription-gated contact reveal + auto-open 1-1 chat (2.5.2)
- Swipe left — Gesture — Skip the current card and show the next
- Bookmark — Icon button — Adds the listing to Favourites (2.6.7)
- Search radius control — Slider — Adjusts geographic coverage (km) — Updates the feed on next load
- Empty state — Display — Conditional (shown when no profiles match) — Illustrative icon/graphic + message: "No profiles match your current criteria"
- Subscription gate — Redirect — On swipe-right without an active subscription, routes to the purchase subscription screen (2.6.13 / 2.6.14)
User Flow:
Screen: Employer's Swipe screen

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated as an Employer.
- The Employer's company location and radius must be set for the feed to activate (2.4.5); if not set ? system prompts the Employer to complete location setup before showing the feed.
- A completed listing is NOT required to browse; swiping is free.
- Subscription status is checked only at the moment of swipe-right (not to view cards).

Employer enters the swipe feed from Home (2.3.1) or after listing setup.
1. System loads the candidate feed in batches of ~20, ordered by the matching algorithm (2.4.3), excluding already-seen cards:
   1.1. If at least one candidate matches the active criteria ? system displays the first card (video + basic info only, no personal contact data).
   1.2. If no candidates match ? system displays the empty state: "No profiles match your current criteria."
2. User swipes left ? system skips the current card, marks it seen (excluded from future loads), and shows the next card.
3. User taps the bookmark icon ? system adds the listing to Favourites (2.6.7) ? system shows a brief confirmation (e.g. "Saved to Favourites").
4. User adjusts the search radius control ? system stores the new radius ? system refreshes the feed on the next load with the updated geographic scope (does not affect the candidate's feed, per 2.4.5).
5. User swipes right ? system checks the Employer's subscription status:
   5.1. If the Employer has an active subscription ? system reveals the candidate's pre-consented contact details (name, email, phone) ? system auto-creates a conversation and opens the 1-1 chat (2.5.2) ? system notifies the candidate (2.7.x).
    + System does NOT require any additional candidate approval (contact is pre-consented at 2.2.4).
   5.2. If the Employer has NO active subscription ? system does NOT reveal contact details ? system does NOT open a chat ? system redirects to the purchase subscription screen (2.6.13 / 2.6.14):
    + If the purchase completes successfully ? system returns the Employer to the Swipe screen ? system reveals the contact and auto-opens the 1-1 chat for that candidate (resumes 5.1).
    + If the purchase is cancelled or fails ? system does NOT reveal contact ? system returns the Employer to the Swipe screen with the card available to swipe again.
6. Personal candidate information is never rendered in the card stack at any point before a successful subscription-gated unlock.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to authenticated, phone-verified Candidates; entered from Home (2.3.1).
- Lets a Candidate browse Employer job postings in a swipe-card interface and express interest for free.
- Each card shows the Employer's video, Company Name, Position name, and Location.
- Swiping right is always free for Candidates.
- Swiping right sends the Candidate's listing/interest to the employer but does NOT initiate a 1-1 chat (chat is created only by an Employer swipe-right with subscription, 2.4.1 / 2.5.1).
- A candidate swipe-right surfaces to the employer in two ways: (a) it appears as a separate "Candidates interested in you" section on the employer's Favourites screen (2.6.7), distinct from the employer's own saved favourites; and (b) it triggers the existing Candidate swipe-right notification (2.7.4), tapping which deep-links the employer into that section. Accessing an interested candidate's contact details still requires an active subscription (same gate as everywhere else).
- As a secondary effect, a candidate's expressed interest acts as a low-priority tie-breaker in the matching algorithm (2.4.3), ranking interested candidates ahead of otherwise-equal matches in the employer's feed.
- Employer contact details are NOT visible to the Candidate from this screen.
- Cards load in batches of ~20; already-swiped cards are excluded from future loads.
- MVP matching is rule-based across all four priority levels (2.4.3), anchored on Location (Priority 1) and Industry/Sector (Priority 2); AI-driven behavioural recommendations are deferred to Phase 2.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Candidate Swipe feed
- Swipe card — Display — Full-screen — Shows: Employer video + Company Name + Position name + Location
  + Employer video — Display — Plays the employer's introduction video
  + Company Name — Display
  + Position name — Display
  + Location — Display — General location (not exact contact data)
- Swipe right — Gesture — Sends the Candidate's listing to the employer (free) — Does NOT open a chat
- Swipe left — Gesture — Skip the current card and show the next
- Bookmark — Icon button — Adds the listing to Favourites (2.6.7)
- Empty state — Display — Conditional (shown when no employer postings match) — Illustrative icon/graphic + message: "No job postings match your current criteria"
User Flow:
Screen: Candidate's Swipe screen

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated as a Candidate with phone verification complete (2.1.8).
- A completed listing is NOT required to browse.
- No subscription is required at any point for Candidates.

Candidate enters the employer-listings swipe feed from Home (2.3.1).
1. System loads the employer feed in batches of ~20, ordered by the matching algorithm (2.4.3), excluding already-swiped cards:
   1.1. If at least one employer posting matches ? system displays the first card (video + company name + position + location).
   1.2. If none match ? system displays the empty state: "No job postings match your current criteria."
2. User swipes left ? system skips the current card, marks it swiped (excluded from future loads), and shows the next card.
3. User taps the bookmark icon ? system adds the posting to Favourites (2.6.7) ? system shows a brief confirmation.
4. User swipes right ? system records the Candidate's interest and sends the Candidate's listing to the employer (free):
   4.1. System does NOT open a 1-1 chat and does NOT reveal employer contact details to the Candidate.
   4.2. System does NOT charge or gate this action — Candidate swipe-right is always free.
   4.3. System surfaces the interest to the employer: it appears in the "Candidates interested in you" section on the employer's Favourites screen (2.6.7), and the system sends the Candidate swipe-right notification (2.7.4) to the employer; tapping that notification deep-links the employer into that section. The employer still needs an active subscription to access the candidate's contact details there.
   4.4. System also passes the interest to the matching algorithm (2.4.3) as a low-priority tie-breaker, promoting this candidate among otherwise-equal matches in the employer's feed.
   4.5. If the send fails due to a network error ? system does NOT mark the card consumed ? system displays a brief retry message: "Couldn't send right now. Please try again."
5. Employer contact details are never shown to the Candidate from this screen.

Description/Asumptions:
- This is a system feature with no dedicated UI screen of its own; it runs server-side when a user opens the swipe feed and determines the order/filtering of listings.
- Profiles are ordered and filtered based on the criteria entered in the listing forms (2.2.3 / 2.2.4); information must be collected for matching to function.
- Priority order (higher takes precedence): Priority 1 — Location (geolocation matching: employer company location vs candidate desired region, with radius filter); Priority 2 — Industry/Sector (employer field of activity vs candidate declared field); Priority 3 — Availability (employer desired start date vs candidate declared availability); Priority 4 — Gender, Age, Contract Type, Language (these four share one priority level).
- For v1 the algorithm is rule-based, not AI-based; AI-driven behavioural recommendation is deferred to Phase 2.
- Tie-breaker (lowest priority, below P1–P4): in the Employer feed, a candidate who has expressed interest (swiped right on this employer, 2.4.2) is ranked ahead of otherwise-equal non-interested candidates.
- Candidates who have not specified a preferred work region are ranked last in the Employer feed rather than excluded entirely (per 2.4.5).
- The Employer radius filter applies only to the Employer feed; it does not affect the Candidate feed.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This is a server-side system feature. No dedicated UI screen is rendered; it produces the ordered/filtered card stack consumed by the swipe feeds (2.4.1 / 2.4.2).
- Input criteria — Data — From listing forms: Location/region, Field of activity, Availability/start date, Gender, Age, Contract type, Language
- Priority weighting — Logic — P1 Location (with radius) > P2 Industry/Sector > P3 Availability > P4 {Gender, Age, Contract Type, Language} (equal weight) > Tie-breaker: candidate expressed interest (2.4.2), Employer feed only
- Output — Ordered, filtered list of listings returned in batches of ~20 to the requesting feed, excluding already-seen/swiped cards
- Fallback rule — Listings missing a preferred work region are ranked last (not excluded)
User Flow:
System Process: Listing matching priority (runs server-side on feed open; feeds 2.4.1 / 2.4.2).

Preconditions:
- Triggered when a user opens the swipe feed (Employer or Candidate).
- Listing criteria from both sides must be collected for matching to function (2.2.3 / 2.2.4).
- For the Employer feed, the Employer's location + radius must be set (2.4.5).
- No AI service is involved — matching is rule-based for v1.

1. User opens the swipe feed ? system requests the next batch (~20) from the matching service.
2. Matching service filters and orders candidate/employer listings by the priority rules:
   2.1. Priority 1 — Location: system filters to listings within the radius (Employer feed) / matching the desired region (Candidate feed):
    + If a listing is outside the radius/region ? system excludes it from the primary set.
    + If a candidate listing has no preferred work region ? system does NOT exclude it ? system ranks it last.
   2.2. Priority 2 — Industry/Sector: within the location-filtered set, system orders by field-of-activity match.
   2.3. Priority 3 — Availability: system further orders by start-date vs availability match.
   2.4. Priority 4 — Gender, Age, Contract Type, Language: system applies these four as equally-weighted tie-breakers.
   2.5. Tie-breaker (Employer feed only) — among otherwise-equal candidates, system ranks those who have expressed interest in this employer (swiped right, 2.4.2) ahead of non-interested candidates.
3. System returns the ordered batch to the feed, excluding already-seen/swiped cards:
   3.1. If the ordered set is empty ? system returns an empty batch ? the feed shows its empty state (2.4.1 / 2.4.2).
   3.2. If the matching service is unreachable ? system does NOT return stale or random cards ? the feed shows a transient error/retry state.
4. AI-driven behavioural recommendation is out of scope for MVP (Phase 2).

Description/Asumptions:
- This is not a standalone screen — it is the location field embedded within the Candidate's listing form (2.2.4).
- Lets the Candidate indicate the region where they wish to work, stored per listing and used as the primary matching criterion (Priority 1, 2.4.3).
- The Candidate enters the region manually via a text search with autocomplete — map interaction is NOT required for the candidate.
- Location autocomplete supports international place names (consistent with the app's international scope).
- [Use my current location] is optional: it pre-fills the field using device GPS if permission is granted; if permission is denied the button is hidden or disabled and the candidate enters location manually.
- Device GPS is used only at the moment the candidate taps [Use my current location] — it is not tracked continuously.
- The candidate's actual physical device location is not used as their preferred work region — only the location they explicitly enter or confirm.
- Location is stored per listing, not per account — a candidate with multiple listings can set a different region for each.
- The field is mandatory: a listing cannot be submitted without a preferred work region.
- Location data is used for matching only and is not displayed to employers as personal contact information.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This feature is embedded within the Candidate's listing set up (2.2.4).
- Preferred work region — Location search input — Required (mandatory) — Text search with autocomplete — Supports international place names
- Use my current location — Button — Optional — Pre-fills the field via device GPS — Hidden/disabled if GPS permission is denied
- Selected location label — Display — Confirmation label shown below the input after a selection
User Flow:
Screen: Preferred work region (embedded in Candidate listing form 2.2.4)

Preconditions:
- User must be an authenticated Candidate completing/editing a listing (2.2.4).
- Location autocomplete service must be reachable.
- GPS permission is NOT required to use this field (manual entry always available).

Candidate reaches the location field in the listing form.
1. Candidate types a city, region, or area name ? system queries the autocomplete service:
   1.1. If matches are found ? system shows autocomplete suggestions as the candidate types (international place names supported).
   1.2. If no matches ? system shows "No matching locations found." and the candidate can refine the text.
   1.3. If the autocomplete service is unreachable ? system still allows the typed text to be confirmed and displays a brief notice that suggestions are unavailable.
2. Candidate selects a suggestion ? system confirms it and displays it as a label below the field ? system saves the location as part of the listing form data (stored per listing).
3. Candidate optionally taps [Use my current location] ? system requests device GPS permission (one-time):
   3.1. If permission is granted ? device returns the current coordinates ? system reverse-geocodes to a place and pre-fills the field (the candidate can still edit it) ? GPS is read once, not tracked continuously.
   3.2. If permission is denied ? system hides or disables [Use my current location] ? the candidate enters the region manually.
4. On form submission the field is validated:
   4.1. If no preferred work region is set ? system does NOT submit the listing ? system displays error: "Please enter your preferred work region."
   4.2. If set ? system stores it and uses it as the Priority 1 matching criterion (2.4.3).
5. System does NOT use the device's physical location as the preferred region and does NOT expose this location to employers as contact information.

Feature/Screen: Employer location input and radius filter
Description/Asumptions:
- This is not a standalone screen — it is the location section embedded within the Employer's preference/listing form (2.2.3).
- Lets the Employer enter their company location on a map and set a radius filter to limit the swipe feed to candidates within a defined distance.
- Company location + radius are stored against the Employer's account preference (not per listing); changing them affects the swipe feed on the next load.
- Location is Priority 1 in the matching algorithm (2.4.3) — the company location and radius are the primary filter on the Employer feed.
- The radius filter applies to the Employer's swipe feed only — it does not affect the Candidate's feed.
- Both company location and radius must be set before the Employer's swipe feed is activated.
- [Use my current location] is optional: pre-fills via device GPS if granted; if denied the Employer enters location manually (button hidden/disabled). GPS is read once on tap, not tracked continuously.
- Location autocomplete supports international place names.
- The map provider is Google Maps Platform (Places Autocomplete + Maps SDK + Geocoding) — chosen for the richest international location data; monthly cost tracked under third-party services.
- The radius slider uses the 2.2.3-confirmed values as the single source of truth: 5–200 km, 5 km steps, default 50 km (the "50 km" reference in the original requirement was illustrative, not a fixed maximum).
- Candidates without a specified preferred work region are ranked last in the Employer feed rather than excluded (per 2.4.3).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This feature is embedded within the Employer's Listing set up (2.2.3) location section.
- Company location — Location search input — Required (mandatory) — Autocomplete — International place names
- Use my current location — Button — Optional — Pre-fills via device GPS — Hidden/disabled if GPS permission is denied
- Map view — Map component — Shows the selected location as a pin — Provider: Google Maps Platform (Maps SDK)
- Radius circle — Map overlay — Updates in real time as the radius slider moves
- Search radius — Slider — Required — Unit km — Range 5–200 km, 5 km steps, default 50 km (per 2.2.3) — Displays the current value (e.g. "50 km")
- Confirmation labels — Display — Selected location and radius value shown below the map
User Flow:
Screen: Company location & radius (embedded in Employer listing form 2.2.3)

Preconditions:
- User must be an authenticated Employer completing/editing the preference form (2.2.3).
- Map and location autocomplete services must be reachable.
- GPS permission is NOT required (manual entry always available).

Employer reaches the location section in the preference form.
1. Employer types the company location ? system shows autocomplete suggestions (international place names):
   1.1. If the autocomplete/map service is unreachable ? system allows manual confirmation of typed text and shows a brief notice that the map/suggestions are degraded.
2. Employer optionally taps [Use my current location] ? system requests device GPS permission (one-time):
   2.1. If granted ? device returns coordinates once ? system places the pin and pre-fills the field (editable); GPS is not tracked continuously.
   2.2. If denied ? system hides/disables the button ? Employer enters location manually.
3. Employer selects a suggestion ? system drops a pin on the map at the selected location.
4. Employer adjusts the [Search radius] slider ? system updates the radius circle around the pin in real time and shows the current value (e.g. "50 km").
5. Employer saves the form ? system validates:
   5.1. If company location is not set ? system does NOT activate the feed ? system displays error: "Please set your company location."
   5.2. If radius is not set ? system does NOT activate the feed ? system displays error: "Please set a search radius."
   5.3. If both are set ? system stores them against the Employer account preference ? the matching algorithm (2.4.3) uses the pin location + radius to filter the Employer swipe feed on the next load.
6. The Employer can update company location and radius at any time ? the change applies to the swipe feed on the next load.
7. This radius affects the Employer feed only; it has no effect on the Candidate feed.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user with at least one conversation; entered from the Chat tab in the bottom navigation (2.3.1).
- Displays all active conversations for the logged-in user as an overview of their messaging activity.
- Both Employers and Candidates see the same conversation-list structure.
- Conversations are auto-created by the system when an Employer swipes right with an active subscription (2.4.1) — users cannot manually create conversations.
- Conversations cannot be deleted or archived by users (not in scope per requirements).
- Default sort: by last-message timestamp, most recent first; pagination applied.
- Search by the other party's name is available.
- Each row shows the other party's name (company name for Employer, first + last name for Candidate), avatar/thumbnail, last-message preview (truncated text or an icon for voice/video/file), last-message timestamp, and an unread indicator if unread messages exist.

- Search bar — Text field — Optional — Placeholder "Search by name" — Filters the list by the other party's name
- Conversation list — List — Required — Sorted by last-message timestamp (descending) — Paginated
  + Other party name — Text — Read-only — Company name (Employer) or first + last name (Candidate)
  + Avatar/thumbnail — Image — Profile thumbnail
  + Last-message preview — Text — Truncated text, or an icon indicating voice/video/file
  + Timestamp — Text — Time of the last message

- Conversation row — Tappable — Navigates to the Conversation box (2.5.2)
User Flow:
Screen: Conversation list

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated (Employer or Candidate).
- Entered via the Chat tab (2.3.1).
- Conversations are system-created only (via Employer swipe-right with active subscription, 2.4.1); the user cannot create one here.

User taps the Chat tab.
1. System loads the user's conversations sorted by last-message timestamp (most recent first), paginated:
   1.1. If at least one conversation exists ? system displays the list with name, avatar, last-message preview, timestamp, and unread indicator where applicable.
   1.2. If no conversations exist ? system displays the empty state: "No conversations yet. When an employer connects with you, your chats will appear here."
2. User scrolls to the end of the loaded page ? system loads the next page (pagination).
3. User types in the search bar ? system filters the list to conversations whose other-party name matches:
   3.1. If matches are found ? system shows the filtered list.
   3.2. If none match ? system shows "No conversations found."
4. User taps a conversation row ? system navigates to the Conversation box (2.5.2) for that conversation and marks its messages as being read on open.
5. System does NOT offer delete or archive actions (out of scope); the user cannot manually create a conversation from this screen.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to either party in an existing conversation; entered by tapping a conversation in the list (2.5.1), tapping [Open chat] from the Employer contact-reveal (2.4.1), or tapping a new-message notification.
- Provides the real-time messaging interface, allowing both parties to exchange text, voice messages, videos, and files.
- All four message types (text, voice, video, files) are available to all users without a subscription.
- File attachments allow: PDF, DOCX, DOC, JPG, JPEG, PNG, max 20 MB per file.
- Voice messages are capped at 2 minutes; video messages are capped at 60 seconds; both are additionally bound by the 20 MB ceiling.
- Real-time delivery uses Firebase Cloud Messaging for push plus Firebase realtime listeners (Realtime Database / Firestore) for live message delivery, typing indicators, and read receipts (upgradeable to a richer chat service such as Stream/Sendbird later). Media (voice, video, files) is stored in cloud object storage (configured in infrastructure).
- Messages display chronologically (newest at the bottom); sent messages on the right, received on the left, each with a timestamp.
- Read receipts: each sent message shows a visual indicator once read by the recipient (applies to all message types).
- Typing indicator: an animated "…" appears at the bottom of the thread in real time when the other party is typing.
- No 'online', 'active now', or 'last seen' status is displayed anywhere in the chat — explicit exclusion.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Conversation box (chat)
- Chat header — Display — Other party's name (company name for Employer, first + last name for Candidate) — No online/last-seen status shown
- Message thread — Scrollable list — Newest at bottom — Sent messages right-aligned, received left-aligned
  + Message bubble — Display — Text / voice / video / file content
  + Timestamp — Text — Per message
  + Read receipt — Indicator — Per sent message, shown once read by the recipient
- Typing indicator — Display — Animated "…" at the bottom of the thread when the other party is typing
- Bottom input bar — Component
  + Write a message… — Text input — Free-text message
  + Microphone — Icon button — Hold to record a voice message (max 2 minutes); release to send/preview
  + Attachment — Icon button — Opens the file picker — Accepted: PDF, DOCX, DOC, JPG, JPEG, PNG — Max 20 MB per file
  + Video — Icon button — Opens camera or gallery for a video message (max 60 seconds)
  + Send — Button — Primary — Sends the composed text message
User Flow:
Screen: Conversation box

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated and a participant in the conversation.
- The conversation must already exist (system-created via 2.4.1) — entered from 2.5.1, the contact-reveal, or a notification.
- A real-time messaging/socket connection and cloud media storage must be available.

User opens a conversation.
1. System loads the message thread (newest at bottom), marks unread received messages as read, and establishes the real-time connection:
   1.1. If the real-time connection succeeds ? system enables live send/receive, typing indicator, and read receipts.
   1.2. If the real-time connection fails ? system still shows the existing thread (read-only fallback) and displays a reconnecting state; sends are queued or blocked with a retry option.
2. User types in [Write a message…] ? system sends a typing signal so the other party sees the "…" indicator in real time; user taps [Send]:
   2.1. If the message sends successfully ? system appends it to the thread (right-aligned) with a timestamp ? recipient's client receives it in real time ? once the recipient reads it, system shows the read receipt on that message.
   2.2. If the send fails (network/socket error) ? system does NOT drop the message ? system marks it as "not delivered" with a retry affordance.
3. User holds the microphone icon to record a voice message ? on release system previews/sends it:
   3.1. System requests microphone permission if not yet granted; if denied ? system does NOT record ? system shows guidance to enable it in Settings.
   3.2. If the recording reaches the 2-minute cap ? system auto-stops recording at 2 minutes.
   3.3. On send ? system uploads the audio to cloud storage and posts it as a voice message; recipient taps to play back.
4. User taps the video icon ? system opens the camera or gallery for a video message:
   4.1. On capture/selection ? if the video exceeds 60 seconds ? system does NOT post it ? system displays: "Video messages must be 60 seconds or less." Otherwise system uploads the video to cloud storage and posts it; recipient taps to play.
   4.2. If camera/gallery permission is denied ? system does NOT proceed ? system shows guidance to enable it in Settings.
5. User taps the attachment icon ? system opens the file picker (PDF, DOCX, DOC, JPG, JPEG, PNG):
   5.1. If the file type is unsupported ? system does NOT attach ? system displays: "Unsupported file type. Allowed: PDF, DOCX, DOC, JPG, JPEG, PNG."
   5.2. If the file exceeds 20 MB ? system does NOT attach ? system displays: "File too large. Maximum size is 20 MB."
   5.3. If valid ? system uploads the file and posts it; recipient taps to open or download.
6. When the other party is typing ? system shows the animated "…" indicator at the bottom of the thread; it disappears when they stop or send.
7. System does NOT display any 'online', 'active now', or 'last seen' status anywhere in the chat.
8. All message types are available without a subscription.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user (Employer or Candidate).
- Entered via Profile screen > Edit profile, or Settings > Manage profile.
- Lets a user view and edit the account-level profile information provided at registration.
- Fields are role-specific: Candidate edits First name, Last name, Phone number (Email read-only); Employer edits Company name (Email read-only).
- Email address is immutable after account creation — displayed for reference but never editable.
- If a Candidate changes their phone number, phone re-verification via SMS OTP is required (reuses 2.1.8 verification).
- Profile data and Listing Form Data are maintained independently — editing the profile does NOT change any listing's contact details.
- The tutorial can also be replayed from Settings — the [Re-access tutorial] entry point lives here and replays the onboarding guide (2.2.1) without changing any other account state.

- Candidate — Last name — Text field — Required — Max 50 characters

- Candidate — Phone number — Text field — Editable — Numeric with country code — Changing it triggers SMS OTP re-verification

2. User edits the editable fields ? user taps [Save] ? system validates:

   2.2. Email is never editable ? system does NOT expose Email for change.
   2.3. Candidate phone-number change — if the phone number was changed ? system does NOT immediately mark it saved ? system sends an SMS OTP to the new number and routes to the phone verification step (reuses 2.1.8):

Description/Asumptions:

- This is the unified entry point that starts listing creation; it routes into the existing role-specific flows rather than duplicating them: form (Employer 2.2.3 / Candidate 2.2.4) ? video (record 2.2.5 or upload 2.2.6, optional teleprompter 2.2.7) ? review ? submit for review.

- Each user may have more than one listing (multiple listings per account are allowed).

- A listing must pass manual Admin moderation (Submit for review ? Pending Review ? Admin queue 3.3.1 / decision 3.3.3) before it becomes visible in the swipe feed; on approval it is published automatically (no AI moderation — that is out of scope).
- At each stage the user can review and edit, save as draft (2.6.5), or proceed.
