- Per-listing actions depend on status: Published ? Edit, Deactivate (moves to Archived); Draft ? Edit, Continue (resumes setup/video, 2.6.6), Delete; Archived ? Restore (back to Published), Delete permanently (2.6.4); Rejected ? Record new video (2.2.5), Upload new video (2.2.6), Delete.
- Deactivating/archiving a listing removes it from the swipe feed immediately.
- Reactivating (restoring) a listing returns it to the swipe feed without requiring re-moderation.
- Each row shows: status badge, video thumbnail (or placeholder), key form-data summary (field of activity, contract type, location), created date, last updated date, and the per-status action buttons.

It will be removed from the feed.") ? on confirm ? system sets status Archived and removes it from the swipe feed immediately ? success message: "Listing deactivated."

Feature/Screen: Archive, restore, and permanently delete listings
Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user; the Archive holds all listings the user has deactivated/archived.
- Entered from Manage Listings (2.6.3) by filtering to Archived, or by deactivating a listing (which moves it here).
- Archived listings are not visible in the swipe feed.
- From the archive the user can Restore a listing or Delete it permanently.
- Restore is a one-tap operation: the listing status changes immediately from Archived to Published and it becomes visible in the swipe feed again. No re-moderation is triggered (the video was previously approved) and there is NO intermediate Inactive/Pending-Activation status.
- To change a listing before making it visible again, the user uses Edit (from the Archived listing's actions in 2.6.3) instead of Restore: editing form data only restores to Published immediately; replacing the video follows the normal video-replacement flow and re-enters Admin review.
- Restoring to Published may trigger "New matching profile" notifications to Employers (2.7.3); employers who already swiped this listing before archiving will not see it again (existing swipe-exclusion logic).
- Permanent deletion is irreversible — the listing and its video are removed from the account entirely and cannot be recovered.
- A confirmation modal is mandatory before permanent deletion, warning that the action cannot be undone.

- Confirm delete — Button — Danger
- Cancel — Button — Secondary

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user; entered from the Profile page > Drafts section.
- A draft is any listing where the form is partially/fully completed but not yet submitted for review/publication — including listings with no video yet.
- Lists all saved drafts (both "Form only" — no video — and "Ready to publish" — form + video saved) together in one section.
- Per-row: listing title / field of activity, draft-type label ("Form only" / "Ready to publish"), date last saved, [Resume] (? 2.6.6), [Delete].
- Default sort: most recently saved date, descending.
- Draft deletion is permanent — the form data and any saved video cannot be recovered; a confirmation prompt is required before deletion ("Are you sure you want to delete this draft? This action cannot be undone." with [Delete] (destructive) and [Cancel]).
- Drafts are private to the account owner and never visible in the swipe feed.
- A draft does not enter moderation until the user explicitly submits it for publication.
- No cap on the number of simultaneous drafts — users may prepare multiple listings at once.
- Auto-save is enabled: the listing form auto-saves after 30 seconds of inactivity, silently creating/updating the draft with a brief subtle "Saved" indicator (small grey text/checkmark near the form title — no modal/toast). An explicit [Save as draft] saves immediately regardless of the timer.
- No draft expiry — drafts persist indefinitely until the user deletes or publishes them (no time-based cleanup).
- Empty state shown when no drafts exist.

  + Date last saved — Text — Format: dd/mm/yyyy
  + Resume — Button — Primary — Opens the draft for continued editing (2.6.6)
  + Delete — Button — Danger — Permanently removes the draft (after confirmation)

   3.1. User taps [Cancel] ? system does NOT delete ? the draft remains.
   3.2. User taps [Delete] ? system permanently removes the draft form data and any saved video (irreversible) ? success message: "Draft deleted." ? system removes the row.

There is no cap on simultaneous drafts and no draft expiry. Drafts auto-save after 30 seconds of inactivity (subtle "Saved" indicator) in the editor (2.6.6).

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user; triggered by [Resume] / [Continue] on a draft (2.6.5 / 2.6.3).
- Returns the user to a saved draft and lets them continue from where they left off: edit the form, add or replace the video, then save as draft again or publish.
- The form loads pre-filled with all previously saved values; all fields are editable, so the user never re-enters data from scratch.
- The video section shows either "No video added yet" (with record/upload options) or an inline preview of the saved draft video with a replace option.
- Resuming does not change the draft's status — it stays a draft until the user explicitly submits for publication.
- Replacing the video does not delete the old one immediately: the new video shows in the preview, but the old video is permanently removed only when the user saves (explicit [Save as draft], [Submit for review], or a 30-second auto-save). If the user navigates back/closes without saving, the new video is discarded and the old video is retained (protects against accidental replacement). There is no version history and no undo — once saved, the old video is deleted from storage (async cleanup). Only one video per listing, 30-second max.
- The editor auto-saves after 30 seconds of inactivity (subtle "Saved" indicator), which also commits a pending video replacement.
- [Publish] / [Submit for review] is enabled only when both form and video are complete; on submit the listing goes to manual Admin moderation (Pending Review ? 3.3.1 / 3.3.3), and on approval is published automatically. The 30-second max video duration applies to any video added here.

  + No video added yet — Display — with [Record] (? 2.2.5) and [Upload] (? 2.2.6) options
  + Saved video preview — Video player — with [Replace video] (replacing permanently removes the old video on save)
- Save as draft — Button — Secondary — Saves current state and returns to the Drafts list (2.6.5)
- Next — Button — Primary — Proceeds to the summary / video step
- Submit for review — Button — Primary — Visible only when both form and video are complete — Submits to manual Admin moderation (3.3.1)

- Triggered by [Resume] (2.6.5) or Draft > [Continue]/[Edit] (2.6.3).
- The selected listing must be in Draft status.

User taps [Resume] on a draft.
1. System loads the listing form pre-filled with all previously saved values; all fields are editable.
2. System shows the video section:
   2.1. If no video was saved ? system shows "No video added yet" with [Record] (2.2.5) and [Upload] (2.2.6).
   2.2. If a video was saved ? system shows the inline preview with [Replace video]. When the user records/uploads a replacement, the new video shows in the preview but the old video is NOT yet deleted — it is permanently removed only on save. One video per listing, 30s max.
3. User edits fields as needed, then chooses one of:
   3.1. [Save as draft] ? system updates the draft (status stays Draft) ? if a video was replaced, system permanently deletes the old video (async storage cleanup) and keeps the new one ? returns to the Drafts list (2.6.5) ? success message: "Draft saved."
   3.2. Add/replace video ? routes to 2.2.5 / 2.2.6, then returns here (replacement committed only on the next save).
   3.3. [Submit for review] (enabled only when form + video are both complete) ? system permanently deletes any replaced old video ? system sets the listing to Pending Review ? sends it to manual Admin moderation (3.3.1; decision 3.3.3) ? on-screen confirmation: "Your listing has been submitted for review."
   3.4. Auto-save (after 30 seconds of inactivity) ? system silently saves the draft and commits any pending video replacement (old video permanently deleted) ? subtle "Saved" indicator.
   3.5. Back / close WITHOUT saving after recording a new video ? system discards the new video and retains the previously saved video (no replacement occurs).
4. On Admin approval ? listing is published automatically and visible in the swipe feed (user notified, 2.7.6). On rejection ? Rejected status, user notified (2.7.6), can record/upload a replacement.
5. Resuming a draft does NOT auto-change its status; it remains a draft until the user explicitly submits for publication.

- Candidates are NOT notified when an Employer adds them to Favourites.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user; entered via Settings > Notifications.
- Lets the user enable or disable push notifications globally within the app, via a single toggle.
- When OFF: the user receives no push notifications, but in-app notifications are still created and visible in the notification centre (2.7).
- When ON: push notifications resume.
- This is an in-app setting only — it does NOT override the device's OS-level notification permission, which the user controls separately in device Settings.
- If the user has denied notification permission at the OS level, the app shows a message directing them to device Settings (the in-app toggle cannot re-grant OS permission).
- The toggle state is stored against the user's account/preferences.

   1.1. If OS-level notification permission is denied ? system shows the OS-permission notice: "Push notifications are turned off for this app in your device settings. Open Settings to enable them." with a shortcut to device Settings ? the in-app toggle reflects that pushes cannot be delivered until OS permission is granted.
   1.2. If OS-level permission is granted ? system shows the toggle in its stored state.
2. User switches the toggle OFF ? system saves the preference ? system stops sending push notifications ? system CONTINUES to create in-app notifications in the notification centre (2.7).
3. User switches the toggle ON ? system saves the preference:
   3.1. If OS-level permission is granted ? system resumes sending push notifications.
   3.2. If OS-level permission is denied ? system does NOT deliver pushes ? system re-shows the OS-permission notice directing the user to device Settings.
4. The in-app toggle never changes the OS-level permission; it only governs the app's own push preference.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user; entered via Settings > Change language.
- Lets the user change the display language of the entire app interface from a searchable list.
- MVP languages: English, German (Swiss / de-CH), French. (German is delivered as Standard German with the Swiss locale de-CH — Swiss spelling conventions, e.g. "ss" not "ß", and CHF formatting; not a written dialect.)

- Currency format display adapts automatically to the device locale / selected language (e.g. CHF for Switzerland, EUR for Germany/France) — format localisation only; real-time exchange-rate conversion is out of MVP scope (Phase 2).
- User-generated content (listing descriptions, chat messages, video subtitles, profile text) is NOT translated — only the app interface changes.
- Additional languages beyond the three are Phase 2 / Change Request items (translation effort and cost); German and French UI translations are a Client deliverable, with development providing the framework and integrating supplied translations.
- App-wide constraint: the entire UI must be localisable via i18next with no hardcoded strings (this global constraint also appears in the gap analysis).

- Language list — List (single select) — Required — Values: English, German (Swiss / de-CH), French — Currently selected language highlighted (check/radio indicator)
- (No explicit Save button — tapping a language applies immediately)
User Flow:
Screen: Change application language

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated.
- Entered via Settings > Change language.
- The selected language preference syncs to the server (with a local fallback if offline).

User opens Change language.

2. User types in the search field ? system filters the list to matching language names.
3. User taps a language ? system applies the change immediately:
   3.1. System updates all UI labels, buttons, and system-generated text to the selected language at once (no app restart).
   3.2. System updates currency formatting to match the locale (e.g. CHF / EUR) — display formatting only, no exchange-rate conversion.
   3.3. System stores the new preference on the server and syncs it across the user's devices:
    + If the server sync fails (offline) ? system still applies the language locally ? system queues the preference to sync when connectivity returns.
4. System does NOT translate user-generated content (listings, chat, profile text, video subtitles) — only the interface changes.
5. Selecting a language that has missing string keys ? system falls back to the default language (English) for those specific keys so the UI never shows blank labels.

Description/Asumptions:
- Employer-only screen; entered via Settings > Manage subscription.
- Lets the Employer view their current subscription details and deep-link to the native store's subscription management page to change or cancel their plan.
- Displays: current plan name (Daily, Monthly, 3-Month, Yearly), status (Active, Expired, Cancelled), start date, and next renewal date (or expiry date if cancelled).
- Plan changes (upgrades/downgrades) and cancellations are NOT handled inside the app — they are managed entirely by the native app store (Apple Settings / Google Play subscriptions); the app provides a deep link only.
- The screen refreshes subscription status from the server on each visit (the server reflects the latest store/receipt state).
- If the Employer has no subscription, this screen routes them to plan selection / purchase (2.6.14).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Manage subscription (Employer-only)
- Current plan — Display (read-only) — Plan name: Daily / Monthly / 3-Month / Yearly
- Status — Badge — Active / Expired / Cancelled
- Start date — Display — Format: dd/mm/yyyy
- Next renewal date / Expiry date — Display — Format: dd/mm/yyyy — Renewal date if Active; expiry date if Cancelled
- Explanatory text — Display — Notes that plan changes and cancellations are managed through the app store
- Manage subscription — Button — Primary — Deep-links to the native store subscription page (Apple Settings / Google Play subscriptions)
- Subscribe / Choose a plan — Button — Primary — Conditional (shown when the user has no active subscription) — Routes to plan selection / purchase (2.6.14)
- Empty/no-subscription state — Display — Conditional — "You don't have an active subscription." + [Choose a plan]
User Flow:
Screen: Manage subscription

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated as an Employer (Employer-only screen).
- If a Candidate reaches this route ? system does NOT show it ? redirects to Home (2.3.1).
- Entered via Settings > Manage subscription.

Employer opens Manage subscription.
1. System refreshes subscription status from the server (latest validated store/receipt state):
   1.1. If the Employer has an active/expired/cancelled subscription on record ? system displays plan name, status badge, start date, and renewal-or-expiry date.
   1.2. If the Employer has no subscription ? system shows the no-subscription state: "You don't have an active subscription." with [Choose a plan] (routes to 2.6.14).
   1.3. If the status refresh fails (network) ? system shows the last-known cached status with a retry option.
2. Employer taps [Manage subscription] ? system deep-links to the native store's subscription management page (Apple Settings / Google Play subscriptions):
   2.1. Plan changes and cancellations happen in the store, outside the app — the app does NOT process them itself.
   2.2. On return to the app, the next status refresh reflects any store-side change.
3. Employer taps [Choose a plan] / [Subscribe] (no active subscription) ? system navigates to the plan selection / In-app purchase flow (2.6.14).

Description/Asumptions:
- Employer-only; triggered by [Subscribe] on the plan selection screen, or by a swipe-right subscription redirect (2.4.1 / 2.6.7).
- Processes the subscription purchase entirely through the native app store billing system — Apple StoreKit (iOS) or Google Play Billing (Android). No alternative payment methods for digital subscriptions; the app never collects or handles card data — the store presents its own native payment sheet.
- Plans: Daily, Monthly, 3-Month, Yearly. Each plan is a separate auto-renewable subscription product created in App Store Connect and Google Play Console (4 products per store); the Client provides the product IDs to the dev team. Prices are configured in the store consoles and fetched/displayed from the store API at runtime — never hardcoded in the app.
- Regional pricing uses the stores' auto-generated regional prices for MVP (rather than manually set per-country prices).
- The plan-selection screen highlights one recommended plan (e.g. a "Best value" badge on Yearly or "Most popular" on Monthly) — the specific badge/plan is a Client choice.
- Free trial: optionally supported by both stores for auto-renewable subscriptions; recommendation is NOT to include a trial at launch (introduce later once baseline conversion is known) — final decision is the Client's.
- Note: prices for each tier are a pending commercial decision by the Client and must be supplied before release.
- Flow: app requests purchase from the native store ? store presents its payment sheet ? Employer authenticates and confirms in the store's native flow ? app receives the purchase receipt ? app sends the receipt to the backend ? backend validates the receipt with Apple/Google ? on success the backend creates a Subscription (status Active, plan type, start date, end date).
- Server-side receipt validation is mandatory to prevent fraud (a client-only "success" is never trusted).
- The flow must handle interruptions gracefully: network failure mid-purchase, user cancellation, pending/deferred payments.
- Duplicate purchase attempts for an already-active subscription are blocked.
- On success: the Employer gains access to candidate contact details via swipe-right for the subscription duration; if the purchase was triggered by a swipe-right redirect, the Employer is returned to the swipe-right flow (2.4.1 / 2.5.2) to complete the contact reveal and conversation creation.
- On error: the subscription is not activated, contact access is not granted, and the Employer remains on the plan selection screen with an error indication.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Plan selection + native store purchase
- Plan options — Option Cards / List (single select) — Values: Daily, Monthly, 3-Month, Yearly — Each shows the store-fetched localised price and billing period — One plan carries a recommended badge ("Best value" / "Most popular", Client choice)
- Subscribe — Button — Primary — Initiates the native store purchase for the selected plan
- Restore purchases — Button — Secondary — Re-validates existing store entitlements (standard store requirement)
- Native store payment sheet — System component (Apple StoreKit / Google Play Billing) — Store-managed UI; the app does NOT render a custom payment form and never handles card data
- Processing state — Display — "Completing your purchase…" while the receipt is validated server-side
- Error state — Display — Conditional — Shown on cancellation/failure/validation error, with a retry affordance
User Flow:
Screen: In-app purchase flow

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated as an Employer.
- Triggered by [Subscribe] on plan selection, or a swipe-right subscription redirect (2.4.1 / 2.6.7).
- The native store (Apple StoreKit / Google Play Billing) must be available; the backend receipt-validation service must be reachable.
- The app never collects card data — payment is confirmed entirely in the store's native sheet.

Employer selects a plan and taps [Subscribe].
1. System checks for an existing active subscription first:
   1.1. If one is already active ? system does NOT start a new purchase ? system displays: "You already have an active subscription." ? routes to Manage subscription (2.6.13).
   1.2. If none active ? system proceeds.
2. System requests the purchase for the selected plan from the native store ? the store presents its native payment sheet:
   2.1. Employer authenticates and confirms payment in the store's native flow.
   2.2. If the Employer cancels in the store sheet ? store returns a cancellation ? system does NOT activate any subscription ? system returns the Employer to plan selection (no error styling beyond a neutral "Purchase cancelled").
3. On confirmation, the store returns a purchase receipt ? system sends the receipt to the backend ? backend validates it with Apple/Google:
   3.1. If the backend validates the receipt successfully ? backend creates a Subscription (status Active, plan type, start date, end date) ? system displays success: "Your subscription is now active." 
    + If the purchase was triggered by a swipe-right redirect ? system returns the Employer to the swipe-right flow (2.4.1 / 2.5.2) to reveal the candidate's contact and create the conversation.
    + Otherwise ? system returns to Manage subscription (2.6.13) showing the active plan.
   3.2. If receipt validation fails (invalid/forged receipt) ? system does NOT activate the subscription ? system does NOT grant contact access ? system shows an error on the plan selection screen and the Employer can retry. A client-side "success" is never trusted without server validation.
   3.3. If the network fails mid-purchase (receipt obtained but not yet validated) ? system does NOT lose the entitlement ? on next launch/refresh the app re-sends the stored receipt for validation (and [Restore purchases] can re-validate) so the Employer is not charged without activation.
   3.4. If the payment is pending/deferred (store-side) ? system shows a pending state and activates only once the store confirms and the receipt validates.
4. Employer taps [Restore purchases] ? system re-validates existing store entitlements and re-activates the subscription record if a valid one exists.
5. On any error the subscription remains inactive and candidate contact access is NOT granted; the Employer stays on plan selection with an error indication.

Description/Asumptions:
- Available to any authenticated user (Employer or Candidate); entered via the Notifications tab / bell icon in the main navigation (2.3.1).
- A centralised in-app screen listing all of the user's notifications, both read and unread.
- Notification types: Employer interest (2.7.2), New matching profile (2.7.3), Candidate swipe-right (2.7.4), New message (2.7.5), Video rejected / Video published (2.7.6).
- Each row shows: a type icon, title, body text, timestamp, and a read/unread visual indicator (bold/highlight for unread).
- Default sort: timestamp descending (most recent first); pagination applied; no search.
- Tapping a notification navigates to its destination (Listing, Chat, swipe feed, Favourites section, etc.) and marks it Read.
- The unread badge count on the bell/tab updates in real time as new notifications arrive.
- In-app notifications are always created and visible here even when push notifications are turned off (2.6.8); push delivery additionally depends on the in-app toggle (2.6.8) and the device OS-level permission.
- Push notifications use the device's default OS notification sound for MVP — the push payload omits any custom-sound field, so iOS/Android play the user's system default. No custom or branded sound, and no per-type sound differentiation (all types share the same default). A custom sound is a post-launch enhancement (would require bundling an audio asset and an app update).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Notification centre
- Notification bell / tab — Icon with badge — In main navigation — Shows the unread count, updated in real time
- Notification list — List — Required — Sorted by timestamp descending — Paginated
  + Type icon — Icon — interest / match / candidate-interest / message / rejection / publication
  + Title — Text — Predefined template text
  + Body — Text — Predefined template text
  + Timestamp — Text — Relative or dd/mm/yyyy hh:mm
  + Read/Unread indicator — Bold / highlight / dot — Unread emphasised
- Empty state — Display — Conditional — "You have no notifications yet."
- Notification row — Tappable — Navigates to the notification's destination and marks it Read
User Flow:
Screen: Notification centre

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated.
- Entered via the Notifications tab / bell icon (2.3.1).

User taps the notification bell / Notifications tab.
1. System loads the user's notifications sorted by timestamp descending, paginated:
   1.1. If at least one notification exists ? system displays the list with type icon, title, body, timestamp, and read/unread indicator.
   1.2. If none exist ? system displays empty state: "You have no notifications yet."
2. User scrolls to the end of the page ? system loads the next page (pagination).
3. User taps a notification ? system marks it Read (removes its unread emphasis), decrements the unread badge, and navigates to its destination:
   3.1. Employer interest (2.7.2) ? Employer's profile/listing.
   3.2. New matching profile (2.7.3) ? the matching candidate listing in the swipe feed.
   3.3. Candidate swipe-right (2.7.4) ? the "Candidates interested in you" section of Favourites (2.6.7).
   3.4. New message (2.7.5) ? the relevant Chat (2.5.2).
   3.5. Video rejected (2.7.6) ? the affected listing showing Rejected status; Video published (2.7.6) ? the now-published listing.
4. As new notifications arrive ? system creates in-app entries here and updates the unread badge in real time, regardless of the push on/off setting.
5. In-app notifications appear here even when push is disabled (2.6.8); only push delivery is affected by the toggle and OS permission.

1. The other party sends a message in a conversation (2.5.2) ? system creates an in-app notification (Unread) in the recipient's centre (2.7.1) with predefined title/body.
2. System decides whether to push:
   2.1. If the recipient is currently viewing the Chat screen of this conversation ? system suppresses the push (the message already appears live) ? the in-app notification is still created.
   2.2. Otherwise, system attempts a push instantly:
    + If push is ON (2.6.8) and OS permission granted ? push delivered instantly.
    + If push is OFF or OS permission denied ? no push ? in-app notification still exists in 2.7.1.
3. Recipient taps the notification (push or in-app) ? system opens the app and navigates directly to that conversation's Chat screen (2.5.2) ? marks the notification Read.
4. Title and body use predefined templates.

Description/Asumptions:
- This is a system-triggered automation (no dedicated screen) targeting the listing owner.
- Notifies a user of the outcome of manual Admin video moderation (3.3) on their submitted listing video — there are two outcomes: Rejected, or Approved/Published. (AI moderation is out of scope; this replaces the original AI-rejection / AI-reversal framing.)
- Rejection: when an Admin rejects the video (3.3.3), the system sends a push instantly AND creates an in-app notification (Unread). Tapping it opens the affected listing in the listings list showing Rejected status, where the user can record/upload a replacement (2.2.5 / 2.2.6). Rejection reasons are NOT disclosed (to avoid gaming the moderation system).
- Approval/Publication: when an Admin approves the video (3.3.3), the listing is published; the system sends a push AND creates an in-app notification (Unread). Tapping it opens the now-published listing.
- Title/body are predefined templates; push delivery depends on the in-app push setting (2.6.8) and OS-level permission; the in-app notification is created regardless.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This is a system-triggered automation. No dedicated UI screen is rendered. Two notification variants:
- Rejection — Trigger: Admin rejects the video (3.3.3) — Push template e.g. Title "Listing not approved", Body "Your listing wasn't approved. Tap to record or upload a new video." (no reason disclosed) — Tap ? affected listing showing Rejected status (record/upload options, 2.2.5 / 2.2.6)
- Approval/Publication — Trigger: Admin approves the video (3.3.3) — Push template e.g. Title "Listing approved!", Body "Your listing has been approved and is now live." — Tap ? the now-published listing
- In-app notification object — Created with status Unread in the user's notification centre (2.7.1) for both variants
User Flow:
System Process: Video validation notice (targets the listing owner; driven by manual Admin moderation 3.3).

Preconditions:
- Triggered by an Admin moderation decision on a submitted video (3.3.3) — either Reject or Approve.
- The push service must be configured.
- No AI involvement — moderation is fully manual (3.3).

Type A — Rejection
1. Admin rejects the video (3.3.3) ? listing status becomes Rejected ? system creates an in-app notification (Unread) in the owner's centre (2.7.1) with a predefined template that does NOT disclose the rejection reason.
2. System attempts a push instantly:
   2.1. If push is ON (2.6.8) and OS permission granted ? push delivered.
   2.2. If push is OFF or OS permission denied ? no push ? in-app notification still exists.
3. User taps the notification ? system opens the affected listing showing Rejected status ? user can record (2.2.5) or upload (2.2.6) a replacement ? notification marked Read.

Type B — Approval / Publication
4. Admin approves the video (3.3.3) ? listing becomes Published and visible in the swipe feed ? system creates an in-app notification (Unread) with a predefined approval template.
5. System attempts a push instantly (same ON/OFF + OS-permission logic as above).
6. User taps the notification ? system opens the now-published listing ? notification marked Read.
7. Both variants use predefined templates; rejection reasons are never disclosed.

Description/Asumptions:
- Web Admin Panel screen; the entry point for Administrators at the Admin Panel URL.
- No authentication required to view the screen — it is the unauthenticated entry point for admins.
- Lets an Administrator securely log in with email and password.
- Admin credentials are managed entirely separately from mobile-app user accounts (separate identity store) — a mobile Employer/Candidate account cannot log in here, and an Admin account cannot log in to the mobile app.
- There is no self-registration for admins — admin accounts are created manually or via a seeding process (see Users management, 3.2).
- After successful login the Administrator is taken to the Admin dashboard.
- A session-inactivity timeout of 30 minutes applies: after 30 consecutive minutes with no interaction, the session is terminated and the Admin is returned to this login screen with "Your session has expired due to inactivity. Please log in again." Any unsaved work (e.g. draft admin notes) is lost.
- Admin password reset is supported: a [Forgot password?] link on this screen sends a single-use reset link to the Admin's registered email via the email delivery service (same mechanism as mobile 2.1.4). The admin reset link expires after 15 minutes (shorter than mobile's 30, reflecting the higher privilege of admin accounts).
- Admin logout is supported: a [Log out] control in the Admin Panel header invalidates the session token and returns to this login screen (no confirmation prompt — accidental clicks are rare on desktop web).
- Failed-login lockout: after 5 consecutive failed attempts the admin account is locked for 30 minutes with "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 30 minutes."; an email alert is sent to all other Admin accounts (security monitoring); the counter resets on successful login or when the lockout expires.
- All connections must use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2 or later.
- Failed login returns a generic message that does not reveal whether the email or the password was wrong.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Admin login (web)
- Email address — Text field — Required — Max 100 characters — Must follow email format — Red border on empty/invalid field when validation is triggered
- Password — Text field (masked) — Required — Show/hide toggle — Red border on empty/invalid field when validation is triggered
- Log in — Button — Primary — Disabled until both fields contain input
- Forgot password? — Hyperlink — Sends a single-use admin reset link (15-min expiry) to the registered email
- Log out — Button — Secondary — In the Admin Panel header (post-login, not on this screen) — Invalidates the session token and returns to login (no confirmation prompt)
User Flow:
Screen: Admin login

Preconditions:
- No authentication required — this is the unauthenticated entry point for Administrators.
- The Admin must have an existing admin account (created via seeding or Users management, 3.2) — no self-registration.
- If a valid admin session already exists ? system skips this screen ? navigates to the Admin dashboard.

Administrator navigates to the Admin Panel URL.
1. System displays the Admin login screen.
2. Admin enters Email address and Password ? Admin clicks [Log in] ? system validates:
   2.1. Validate required fields:
    + If Email is empty ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? system displays error: "Email is required."
    + If Password is empty ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? system displays error: "Password is required."
   2.2. Validate email format (system checks input against standard email format — contains @ and a valid domain):
    + If format is invalid ? system does NOT query the backend ? system displays error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   2.3. Validate credentials against the separate admin identity store (NOT the mobile user store):
    + If credentials are valid ? backend returns success ? system resets the failed-attempt counter ? system creates an admin session ? system navigates the Administrator to the Admin dashboard.
    + If the email is not found in the admin store OR the password does not match ? backend returns failure ? system does NOT create a session ? system increments the failed-attempt counter ? system displays generic error: "Invalid email or password."
    + If a mobile-app (Employer/Candidate) account attempts to log in here ? it is not in the admin store ? system does NOT authenticate ? same generic error: "Invalid email or password."
    + If the backend is unreachable ? system does NOT authenticate ? system displays error: "Network error. Please try again."
   2.4. Validate lockout state (system checks consecutive failed attempts for this admin):
    + If the admin has reached 5 consecutive failed attempts ? system does NOT attempt authentication ? account is locked for 30 minutes ? system displays error: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 30 minutes." ? system sends an email alert to all other Admin accounts.
    + The counter resets on a successful login or when the 30-minute lock expires.
3. Admin clicks [Forgot password?] ? system sends a single-use reset link (15-minute expiry) to the admin's registered email via the email service (same mechanism as 2.1.4):
   3.1. System always shows a neutral confirmation regardless of whether the email is a known admin: "If an admin account exists for this email, a reset link has been sent."
   3.2. Admin opens the link ? if valid, unused, and within 15 minutes ? system shows the set-new-password screen (admin password policy) ? on success, old password invalidated, link marked used. If expired/used ? "This link has expired. Please request a new password reset."
4. After login, a 30-minute session-inactivity timeout applies; on timeout ? system ends the admin session ? returns the Administrator to this login screen with: "Your session has expired due to inactivity. Please log in again." (unsaved work is lost).
5. Admin clicks [Log out] (header) at any time ? system invalidates the session token ? returns to the login screen (no confirmation prompt).
6. There is no self-registration and no public sign-up link on this screen.

Description/Asumptions:
- Web Admin Panel screen; entered by clicking a user in the list (3.2.1).
- Shows the full profile and activity of a single user and provides admin actions: Suspend, Reactivate, Delete, and Export user data.
- Displays: all registration fields, role, account status, registration date, phone-verification status (Candidate only); a listing summary (counts of Published, Draft, Archived, Rejected); and subscription status (Employer only: plan, status, start/end dates).
- Suspend ? sets status Suspended and hides all the user's listings from swipe feeds (listings are not deleted); the suspended user cannot create new listings or swipe. Existing conversations and favourites are preserved and remain fully accessible to BOTH parties (the other party can still message the suspended user; the suspended user can still read and receive messages). The other party is NOT notified of the suspension and no "user suspended" message appears in the chat (privacy / FADP — account status is personal data). Reactivate ? restores a Suspended user to Active and makes listings visible again.
- Delete ? permanently removes the user and their personal data (profile/thumbnail, contact details, listing data) — irreversible. In existing conversations, the deleted user's message TEXT is retained for the other party, but their name is replaced with "Deleted account" (generic placeholder avatar) in the chat header and bubbles; the conversation becomes read-only for the other party (input/send disabled) with a footer note "This account is no longer available"; the other party can still scroll/read history. The other party is NOT push/in-app notified — the "Deleted account" label is the only indicator.
- Suspension is reversible; deletion is permanent and irreversible — both require a confirmation, and Delete requires an explicit irreversibility warning.
- Export user data ? CSV of this user's profile (User ID, name/company name, email, role, registration date, account status); Super Admin only. Full export incl. messages/videos is out of MVP scope.
- All admin actions on a user account are logged with admin ID and timestamp for audit.

- Subscription status — Display — Employer only — Plan, status, start date, end date
- Suspend account — Button — Danger — Sets status Suspended, hides listings (with confirmation)
- Reactivate account — Button — Primary — Conditional (shown when Suspended) — Restores to Active
- Delete account — Button — Danger — Permanent delete (with irreversible-delete confirmation)
- Export user data — Button — Secondary — Super Admin only — Downloads this user's CSV

Popup: Delete account confirmation
- Warning — Text — "This permanently deletes the user and their data (profile, listings, videos, contact details). Existing chats stay readable for the other party but the user appears as 'Deleted account'. This cannot be undone."
- Confirm delete — Button — Danger
- Cancel — Button — Secondary

- Admin must be authenticated (3.1.1).
- Entered by selecting a user from 3.2.1.
- [Export user data] is visible only to Super Admin.

Admin opens a user's detail screen.

2. Admin clicks [Suspend account] ? system asks for confirmation ("Suspend this account? All their listings will be hidden from the feed.") :
   2.1. On confirm ? system sets status Suspended ? hides all the user's listings from swipe feeds (not deleted) ? the user can no longer create listings or swipe ? existing conversations and favourites are preserved and stay fully usable for both parties ? system does NOT notify the other chat party and does NOT insert any "suspended" system message ? logs the action (admin ID + timestamp) ? success message: "Account suspended."
   2.2. On cancel ? no change.
3. Admin clicks [Reactivate account] (Suspended users) ? system sets status Active ? makes the user's listings visible again ? logs the action ? success message: "Account reactivated."
4. Admin clicks [Delete account] ? system opens the irreversible-delete confirmation:
   4.1. On [Cancel] ? system does NOT delete.
   4.2. On [Confirm delete] ? system permanently removes the user and their personal data (profile/thumbnail, contact details, listing data) ? in existing conversations the deleted user's message text is retained but their identity is replaced with "Deleted account" (placeholder avatar), and the conversation becomes read-only for the other party (input/send disabled, footer "This account is no longer available") ? the other party is NOT notified ? logs the action ? success message: "Account permanently deleted." ? system returns to the user list (3.2.1). This cannot be undone.
5. Super Admin clicks [Export user data] ? system generates a CSV of this user's profile fields (User ID, name/company name, email, role, registration date, account status) ? browser downloads it ? logs the export (admin ID + timestamp).
   5.1. If a non-Super-Admin triggers export ? system does NOT generate the file ? permission error.
6. Every administrative action (suspend/reactivate/delete/export) is recorded with admin ID and timestamp for audit.

Description/Asumptions:
- This is not a standalone screen — it is a reusable date-range component embedded at the top of every report screen (3.5.2–3.5.6).
- Lets the Admin control the time period of data shown on any report.
- Components: "From" date input, "To" date input, [Apply], quick-select shortcuts (Last 7 days / Last 30 days / Last 3 months / Last 12 months), a label showing the active range, and a [Refresh] button.
- All reports default to the last 30 days on initial load.
- "To" date cannot be in the future; "From" date cannot be later than "To" — validation enforced on Apply.
- Refresh is per admin user per report screen (two admins can refresh the same report independently); auto-refresh is NOT supported — data updates only on manual refresh or initial page load.
- All report screens are read-only and accessible to all admin roles unless stated otherwise; no report data can be modified.
- All data tables within reports are paginated.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This component is embedded at the top of every report screen (3.5.2–3.5.6). No standalone screen of its own.
- From date — Date input — Required — Format: dd/mm/yyyy — Cannot be later than To
- To date — Date input — Required — Format: dd/mm/yyyy — Cannot be a future date
- Quick-select shortcuts — Buttons/Chips — Last 7 days / Last 30 days / Last 3 months / Last 12 months
- Active-range label — Display — Shows the currently applied range
- Apply — Button — Primary — Applies the range and refreshes the report
- Refresh — Button — Secondary — Re-pulls data for the current range; shows a last-refreshed timestamp
User Flow:
Component: Date range selection (embedded in every report screen).

Preconditions:
- Admin must be authenticated (3.1.1) and on a report screen (3.5.2–3.5.6).

Admin opens any report screen.
1. System loads the report with the default range = last 30 days and renders all charts/KPIs/tables for that range.
2. Admin picks a custom range via the From/To inputs OR taps a quick-select shortcut (Last 7 days / 30 days / 3 months / 12 months).
3. Admin clicks [Apply] ? system validates the range:
   3.1. If To is a future date ? system does NOT apply ? system displays error: "The 'To' date cannot be in the future."
   3.2. If From is later than To ? system does NOT apply ? system displays error: "The 'From' date cannot be after the 'To' date."
   3.3. If valid ? system refreshes all charts, KPI cards, and tables on the report to reflect the selected range, and updates the active-range label.
4. Admin clicks [Refresh] ? system re-pulls data for the current range and updates the last-refreshed timestamp (refresh is per admin per report; no auto-refresh).
5. All report screens are read-only — no data can be modified from any report.

Description/Asumptions:
- Web Admin Panel report; entered from the Reports section.
- Shows new user registration activity within the selected date range (3.5.1), broken down by role and platform, to monitor growth and acquisition.
- KPI cards: total new candidate registrations, total new employer registrations (in period).
- Line chart "New registrations over time" — two lines (Candidates, Employers); daily granularity for ranges ? 30 days, weekly for > 30 days.
- Horizontal bar chart "Top 10 countries by new registrations".
- Pie chart "Registration split by platform" (iOS / Android).
- Country is determined at registration time, captured once and stored on the User record (it answers "where did the user register from", not where they are now). Primary method: the device locale's country component (e.g. en_CH ? CH) — a free, permission-free OS call. Fallback: IP geolocation (e.g. MaxMind GeoLite2) only when the locale has no country component. Users with neither are grouped as "Unknown".
- Deleted accounts are excluded from registration counts.
- All data is read-only; accessible to all admin roles.

Description/Asumptions:
- Web Admin Panel report; entered from the Reports section.
- Shows swipe activity across the platform within the selected date range (3.5.1) — engagement volume, direction trends, and subscription conversion at the swipe level.
- KPI cards: total swipes, total left swipes, total right swipes, overall right-swipe rate (%).
- Stacked bar chart "Swipe volume over time by direction" (Left / Right).
- Pie chart "Right swipe conversion" (Contact revealed / Redirected to subscription purchase).
- Horizontal bar chart "Top 10 fields of activity by right swipe count".
- Right-swipe rate = (total right swipes / total swipes) × 100.
- Both employer-on-candidate and candidate-on-employer swipes count toward total swipes.
- A right swipe is classified "Contact revealed" only if the employer had an active subscription at the exact time of the swipe (candidate right-swipes, being free and not contact-revealing, fall under the non-revealed/other bucket).
- All data is read-only; accessible to all admin roles.

Description/Asumptions:
- Web Admin Panel report; entered from the Reports section.
- Shows subscription acquisition activity within the selected date range (3.5.1) — volume of new subscriptions, plan-preference distribution, and acquisition trends.
- KPI cards: total active subscriptions (point-in-time, as of the report end date), new subscriptions acquired in period, breakdown by plan type (Daily / Monthly / 3-Month / Yearly).
- Line chart "New subscription acquisitions over time".
- Grouped bar chart "New subscriptions by plan type".
- "Total active subscriptions" is a point-in-time count as of the report end date, NOT all subscriptions created in the period.
- The four plan types are the only ones in scope.
- The Admin Panel shows subscription COUNT data only for MVP — no revenue amounts, currency values, or financial totals. Actual revenue is viewed in the store dashboards (App Store Connect > Sales and Trends; Google Play Console > Financial reports), which are authoritative and provide commission/tax/payout detail.
- No direct financial-data integration for MVP (integrating the Apple/Google financial APIs — OAuth per store, multi-currency, commission/tax/refund logic — is disproportionate to MVP value). Post-MVP, an estimated gross-revenue column can be derived cheaply as count × configured plan price (an estimate that ignores refunds/regional/promo pricing); exact revenue stays in the store dashboards.
- Subscription data is sourced from the Apple/Google IAP APIs, so the available detail depends on what those APIs expose.
- All data is read-only; accessible to all admin roles.

Description/Asumptions:
- Web Admin Panel report; entered from the Reports section.
- Shows subscription cancellation and expiry activity within the selected date range (3.5.1) — churn volume, trends, and which plan types are most affected.
- KPI cards: total cancellations in period, total expirations in period, overall churn rate (%) for the period.
- Line chart "Cancellations and expirations over time" (two lines: Cancellations / Expirations).
- Horizontal bar chart "Churn by plan type" (Daily / Monthly / 3-Month / Yearly).
- Churn rate = (cancellations + expirations) / total active subscriptions at start of period × 100.
- A subscription that expires and is immediately renewed is NOT counted as churn.
- Data source (confirmed feasible): cancellation and expiry events come from the stores' real-time subscription webhooks — Apple App Store Server Notifications V2 (DID_CHANGE_RENEWAL_STATUS ? cancellation intent; EXPIRED ? expiry; REVOKE ? refund) and Google Play Real-time Developer Notifications via Cloud Pub/Sub (SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELED; SUBSCRIPTION_EXPIRED). A cancellation is counted at the time the user turns off auto-renew (intent), giving early visibility, while the subscription stays Active in the system until the period actually ends.
- DEPENDENCY: this report consumes data captured by the subscription webhook handler. That handler is not an existing WBS feature — without it, no cancellation/expiry events are stored and the churn report (and renewal/expiry status accuracy) would have no data. The churn report and the webhook handler must be built together.
- All data is read-only; accessible to all admin roles.

- Cancellation/expiry events are sourced from the store webhooks (Apple ASSN V2 / Google RTDN), captured by the subscription webhook handler (a dependency that must exist for this report to have data).

Admin opens the Subscription churn report.

2. System computes:
   2.1. KPI cards: total cancellations, total expirations, and churn rate = (cancellations + expirations) / active subscriptions at start of period × 100. A cancellation is counted at auto-renew-off intent; the subscription remains Active until the period ends.
   2.2. Line chart: cancellations and expirations over time (two series).
   2.3. Bar chart: churn by plan type.
   2.4. A subscription that expires and is immediately renewed is NOT counted as churn.
   2.5. If the webhook handler is not yet capturing events ? the report renders empty (no churn data).
3. Admin changes the date range (3.5.1) ? [Apply] ? all KPIs/charts refresh.
4. If no churn data in range ? empty states / zero KPIs.
5. The report is read-only.
